topic: Topic: Nanomaterial-Based Catalysis, Adsorption, and Membrane Technology for Elimination of Emerging Contaminants from Water
summary:
Water security is paramount for human survival and societal progress. However, rapid technological advancements have led to the emergence of various contaminants in water, posing significant threats to both human health and the environment. These emerging contaminants, such as pharmaceutical residues, microplastics, heavy metals, and other novel pollutants, introduce new challenges due to their potential adverse effects on ecosystems and human health.

Addressing these challenges requires innovative solutions. In this context, there has been considerable interest in novel materials, particularly those demonstrating nanoscale properties, to tackle water contamination issues. These materials include an array of significant nanomaterials widely utilized in water treatment:
● Metal-Organic Frameworks (MOFs);
● Covalent Organic Frameworks (COFs);
● Graphene;
● Carbon Nanofibers;
● Nanosilver;
● Nanoscale Iron;
● Nanoscale Iron Oxides;
● Carbon Nanotubes;
● Titanium Dioxide Nanoparticles;
● Zinc Oxide Nanoparticles;
● Silicon Nanoparticles;
● Nanoscale Aluminum Oxides;
● Nanoscale Nickel;
● Cerium Oxide Nanoparticles;
● Zinc Oxide Nanocomposites;
● Nanoscale Perovskites;
● Nanoscale Titanomagnetites;
● Nanoscale Silica Wax;
● Nanoscale Bentonite;
● Nanoscale Zinc Oxides.

These nanomaterials play pivotal roles in water treatment and contaminant removal. Our focus lies in exploring the practical application of these nanoscale materials in water treatment to combat emerging contaminants. This involves investigating how these materials can effectively address various water pollutants, including pharmaceutical residues, microplastics, heavy metals, and more, which can cause severe harm to ecosystems and human health.

With this objective in mind, our Special Issue titled 'Nanomaterial-Based Catalysis, Adsorption, and Membrane Technology for Elimination of Emerging Contaminants from Water' aims to showcase the effectiveness of nanoscale catalysts, adsorbents, and membrane materials. We invite researchers and experts to contribute their studies, innovative experiments, or comprehensive reviews on the efficacy of these materials in eliminating emerging contaminants from water. We anticipate global contributions to advance the application and development of these nanomaterials in the field of water treatment and environmental preservation.
keywords: ["​Water contaminants", "nanotechnology", "water purification", "advanced catalysts", "adsorption technology", "membrane filtration", "emerging pollutants", "nanomaterial applications", "water treatment innovation", "environmental remediation"]

topic: Topic: Noble Metal-based Nanomaterials and Nanostructures for Next-Generation Energy Conversion and Storage
summary:
In the quest for a sustainable future, materials science has emerged as a pivotal frontier, enabling transformative advancements in energy conversion and storage technologies. The advancement of sustainable and renewable energy conversion and storage technologies with remarkable efficiency is now considered more crucial than ever. From this perspective, the exploration of efficient and eco-friendly materials for electrochemical energy conversion and storage represents a potential research area for developing sustainable energy resources.

Advances in nanomaterial synthesis and its usage in energy conversion and storage have attracted noteworthy attention from researchers worldwide due to their interesting outcomes. In this context, tuning the physicochemical properties of the nanostructures and nanomaterial significantly increases the electrochemical performance in various energy-related devices. The unique properties of nanostructures and nanomaterial, such as electrical conductivity, porosity, topography, surface area to volume ratio, and nature/type of active centers, directly affect the charge/mass-transfer kinetics, reaction kinetics, stability, storage capacity, and ultra-long cycle life of these electrochemical energy conversion and storage devices. Noble metals, such as palladium (Pd), platinum (Pt), gold (Au), ruthenium (Ru), rhodium (Rh), Iridium (Ir), osmium (Os), and silver (Ag), have positioned themselves as promising candidates in the realm of synthesizing nanomaterials and nanostructures for energy conversion and storage due to their unique characteristics that enhance performance efficiency and durability in various energy-related applications.

In this Special Issue, we will mainly discuss the noble metal-based nanomaterials and nanostructures for next-generation energy conversion and storage technologies, along with an in-depth understanding of the mechanisms involved in these electrochemical systems. Then, the details of the various strategies for controllable and tuned synthesizing of noble metal-based nanoscale advanced materials with unique morphologies and inimitable properties for various high-performance energy conversion reactions and energy storage systems will be concluded.

Lastly, we will summarize the critical challenges in designing noble metal-based nanostructures with adjustable and identified active sites, providing a comprehensive guide in characterization methodologies to explore mechanistic kinetics and, notably, the potential new design and development-related future opportunities.
keywords: ["Noble metal-based nanostructures", "catalysis", "energy conversion", "batteries", "supercapacitors"]

topic: Topic: Autonomous Driving and Intelligent Vehicle Highway Systems
summary:
In an era of rapid technological advancement, Autonomous Driving and Intelligent Vehicle Highway Systems (AD-IVHS) stand at the forefront of innovation, reshaping the transportation landscape. As we witness the convergence of cutting-edge technologies, from artificial intelligence (e.g., Large Language Models (LLM), ChatGPT, and Transformers) and sensor networks to connectivity and robotics, now is the opportune time for a comprehensive exploration of their integration into the realm of autonomous vehicles and intelligent highway systems.

This Special Issue aims to bring together researchers, academics, and industry experts to contribute their latest findings and insights into the multifaceted aspects of AD-IVHS. We invite original research articles, review papers, and case studies that delve into the challenges, opportunities, and breakthroughs in this transformative field. Researchers are encouraged to submit high-quality contributions, providing a platform for the dissemination of state-of-the-art research that will propel the development and deployment of autonomous driving and intelligent transportation systems. Topics of interest include, but are not limited to:
● Intelligent decision-making, planning, and control for autonomous driving;
● Sensing and perception algorithms and systems;
● Dynamic environment understanding;
● Test and evaluation of algorithms, approaches, and frameworks;
● Human-autonomy interactions;
● V2X systems;
● Connected automated vehicles;
● Social interactions for autonomous driving;
● Driver behavior and human factors;
● Application of LLM for AD-IVHS;
● Intelligent communication (V2I and V2V);
● Safety of the intended function and functional safety.
keywords: ["Autonomous driving", "sensing", "perception algorithms", "Human-autonomy interactions", "driver behavior", "connected automated vehicles", "Intelligent communication (V2I and V2V)", "V2X systems", "intelligent vehicle highway systems"]

topic: Topic: Intelligent, Safe, and Green Shipping-oriented Maritime Data Exploitation and Knowledge Discovery
summary:
Economic globalization has fostered the development of intelligent navigation and automated container terminals. It is important to recognize that smart and green shipping will shape future shipping variation tendencies by considering safety, cost, and efficiency factors. Automatic identification system (AIS) data is commonly used to aid real-world ship navigation. Indeed, varied maritime data sources (ship-borne surveillance video, port surveillance video, Lidar, GPS, etc.) have been introduced to enhance shipping safety and efficiency. This approach facilitates the collection of sufficient data for conducting smart shipping-related studies.

It is noted that ships in the smart shipping era can find optimal traveling trajectories by exploiting historical yet large-scale AIS data. Moreover, energy consumption in the smart ship era is supposed to be significantly lower than in the traditional shipping era. For instance, cargo will be transmitted by autonomous guided vehicles (AGV) powered by green energy. Meanwhile, safety can be enhanced in the smart shipping era as potential accidents in sea and port areas will be easily identified with the help of AI techniques. It is necessary to exploit shipping-related knowledge from large-scale maritime data, which can be further used to optimize shipping energy consumption. With that aim in mind, we invite full paper contributions that match the general theme of "Intelligent, Safe, and Green Shipping-oriented Maritime Data Exploitation and Knowledge Discovery". We also encourage submissions from a broad range of research fields related to the relevant issues. Some potential exemplary topics are shown as follows (but not limited to):
● Energy efficiency-based ship trajectory optimization using large-scale AIS data;
● Maritime traffic safety-based shipping knowledge discovery;
● Ship autonomous navigation employing multiple maritime data sources;
● Analysis of energy consumption reduction in the smart shipping era;
● Optimization of scheduling tasks for AGVs, shore bridges, and yards in automated container terminals;
● Intelligent maritime data mining, etc.
keywords: ["Ship autonomous navigation", "maritime knowledge discovery", "shipping energy consumption", "intelligent maritime data exploitation", "intelligent AGV path optimization"]

topic: Topic: Membrane Technologies for the Removal of Emerging Contaminants from Water
summary:
The rising concerns regarding microplastics, endocrine disruptors, and perfluoroalkyl substance pollution have garnered widespread public attention. Membrane technology has emerged as a leading solution in both drinking water and wastewater treatment. Its large-scale application in engineering largely hinges on three key factors: the preparation of membrane materials, the development of membrane processes, and the effective management of membrane fouling. By employing membrane technology in wastewater treatment, significant strides are made towards achieving sustainable development goals in water use, thereby curbing water demand.

This Special Issue of "Membrane Technology and Water Treatment" will bring together articles on new developments in membrane fabrication and their application within water treatment processes. The primary goal is to assess the current landscape and catalyze future innovations in the field of pioneering membrane-assisted processes and technologies in the water sector. Topics include, but are not limited to, emerging water treatment technologies, inventive process design, modeling and validation, life cycle assessment, and techno-economic analysis. Original papers and reviews are welcome.
keywords: ["Membrane separation", "new pollutants", "nanofiltration/microfiltration/ultrafiltration", "emerging water pollutants", "micro/nano plastics", "recyling", "innovative membrane technology", "forward osmosis", "reverse osmosis", "wastewater treatment"]

topic: Topic: Innovative Technologies for Pollution Control and Environmental Remediation
summary:
The increasing population, industrial development, and urbanization expansion have escalated concerns surrounding water pollution, treatment, and management. Nowadays, "new" pollutants pose a significant challenge as existing technologies struggle to effectively eliminate these substances, leading to potentially severe consequences for both the aquatic environment and human health. Therefore, there is an urgent need to develop innovative water purification and wastewater treatment technologies to ensure environmental and economic sustainability.

This Special Issue is dedicated to exploring "Chemical oxidation/advanced oxidation processes (AOP) for pollution control and environmental remediation." Its focus is on presenting cutting-edge, ongoing research that contributes to the innovative development and field application of chemical oxidation and AOPs in environmental contexts and for reuse purposes. Priority topics covered in this Special Issue include, but are not limited to: i) chemical oxidation and design of AOPs; ii) novel materials; iii) new catalysts; iv) pilot trials; v) development of new processes; vi) mechanisms; vii) applications in wastewater treatment and air purification.
keywords: ["Water and Wastewater Treatment", "chemical oxidation", "advanced oxidation processes", "photocatalysis", "peroxymonosulfate activation", "catalytic mechanism", "advanced technology", "micro/nano plastics", "contaminants of emerging concern and priority pollutants", "monitoring of water", "energy/resource recovery", "biological treatment"]

topic: Topic: Neuroimaging Advances in Cognitive Aging and Alzheimer's Disease
summary: Advancements in multimodal neuroimaging techniques, such as MRI and PET, have significantly accelerated brain structural and functional research concerning cognitive aging and Alzheimer's disease (AD), offering new insights into understanding the neural mechanisms underlying age-related cognitive changes in both normal and pathological aging. Particularly noteworthy is the fusion of the big data of multimodal imaging with AI techniques, enabling the potential prediction of AD risk and the assessment of brain health in elderly individuals. This facilitates the development of important predictive models and imaging biomarkers for future clinical applications. This Special Issue aims to present the latest breakthroughs in neuroimaging, focusing on their impact on unraveling neural mechanisms, developing computational models, and identifying imaging biomarkers of cognitive aging and AD, ultimately paving the way for enhanced clinical evaluations and innovative therapeutic interventions.
keywords: ["Neuroimaging", "cognitive aging", "Alzheimer’s disease", "MRI", "PET", "machine learning", "AI"]

topic: Topic: Operating Room Video Recording : the Path to More Powerful Surgomics
summary: This Special Issue is dedicated to delving into the regulatory, technical, and legal aspects surrounding routine video recording in operating rooms. It encompasses various considerations concerning the benefits and challenges associated with the widespread adoption of video recording in surgical settings. Particularly, priority will be given to papers that address the burgeoning field of surgery/surgiomics/surgeomics for publication in this Special Issue. Authors are encouraged to probe the evolving landscape of video recording in surgery, unraveling its potential benefits while navigating the intricate web of associated challenges.
keywords: ["Routine video recording", "operating room", "legal dimensions", "surgery", "surgiomics", "surgeomics"]

topic: Topic: Exposure to Environmental Pollutants on Child Health
summary:
Children represent the future of society, yet their health and development face potential threats from environmental pollution. This Special Issue seeks to delve deeply into the impact of environmental pollutants on children's well-being, with a particular focus on their exposure to contaminants in air, water, food, etc. By doing so, we aim to foster a comprehensive understanding of the challenges impacting children's health. Our goal is to spotlight this pressing issue and encourage interdisciplinary collaboration to further study and effectively respond to child health-related concerns.

Potential topics include but are not limited to:
● Air pollution exposure and respiratory health;
● Water pollution and neurodevelopment;
● Food contamination and growth and development of children;
● Detailed analysis of children's exposure to chemicals through food, emphasizing the potential harm of food contamination to their growth, development, and immune systems;
● Threats from emerging contaminants, focusing on emerging pollutants, paying special attention to children's exposure to plastic particles, drug residues, etc., and exploring the new threats they may pose to children's health;
● Socioeconomic Inequality and Environmental Justice: Investigate focusing on the unequal exposure of children to environmental pollution due to socioeconomic factors, leading to varying health impacts in different social groups.

Our objective is to present extensive research on children's exposure to environmental pollutants and related health effects. By providing robust scientific data, we aim to support global health and environmental initiatives, striving together to create a cleaner and safer environment that ensures every child’s right to a healthy upbringing. We cordially welcome contributions from all scholars and researchers in these fields for publication in this Special Issue.

All submissions MUST consider the implications for human and/or wildlife exposure to contaminants. Please see the Aims and Scope of JEEA for further guidance.
keywords: ["Environmental pollution", "air pollution", "exposure", "water pollution", "children's growth", "immune systems", "drug residues", "chemicals"]

topic: Topic: Cerebellum and Cognitive Functions
summary: The cerebellum, traditionally known for its pivotal role in motor control, has recently emerged as a key player in diverse cognitive functions. These encompass language, working memory, perception, executive functions, behavioral control, and social cognition. While the conventional perspective implicates that the cerebellum collaborates with brain regions conventionally tied to cognitive processes—such as the cerebral cortex, hippocampus, and ventral tegmental area—a mounting body of research, including clinical, pathological, neurophysiological, and functional neuroimaging findings, underscores a direct link between the cerebellum and cognitive functions. Given that cognitive impairment stands out as a prevalent and challenging symptom in neurodegenerative diseases and aging-related diseases, a deeper comprehension of the intricate connections between the cerebellum and cognitive functions holds potential implications for innovative treatments.
keywords: ["​Cerebellum", "cognitive functions", "neurodegenerative diseases", "aging"]

topic: Topic: Micro-nano Structure Design and Manufacturing Technology
summary:
Micro-nanostructuring is a nanoscale technology that can be used to design, develop, and manufacture tiny structures and mechanical devices. Micro-nano structures refer to the use of appropriate atomic-level components combined with precisely controlled mechanical technology to design, manufacture, and improve microstructures and mechanical systems at the atomic scale. It employs micron-scale structures to realize various physical phenomena, such as converting energy, storing energy, detecting radiation, etc. Therefore, micro/nanostructures possess unique optical, electrical, magnetic, and thermal properties. Research on the preparation of micro-nano structures has important research value and broad development prospects. In addition, micro-nano structure manufacturing technology conforms to concepts such as environmental protection, sustainable development, and green manufacturing.

A variety of micro-nano structure preparation technologies have been developed, including photolithography technology, electron beam lithography technology, focused ion beam technology, nanoimprint technology, etc. Micro-nano functional devices have attracted widespread attention in cutting-edge research fields, such as micro-nano optics, micro-nano sensors, micro-electromechanical systems, and biomedicine, due to their deep integration, intelligence, and miniaturization characteristics.

This Special Issue aims to jointly explore research on the latest technologies related to "micro-nano structures", including upgraded or innovative technical means to achieve micro-structure manufacturing, the latest advances and technologies in additive manufacturing of micro-nano functional devices, manufactured micro/nano functional devices, etc. Everyone is welcome to submit.
keywords: ["Micro-nano structure", "micro-nano manufacturing technology", "micro-nano functional components", "micro-nano optics", "micro-nano sensors", "micro-electromechanical systems", "sustainable development", "green manufacturing"]

topic: Topic: Surface and Interface Chemistry Applied to Mineral Separation and Element Extraction
summary:
The interdisciplinary nature of surface and interface chemistry makes it a key enabler for developing innovative strategies to address the challenges associated with complex ore bodies and dwindling high-grade mineral resources. By delving into the fundamental principles of surface and interface chemistry that govern the complex interactions between minerals, reagents, and solutions, a deeper understanding of the underlying mechanisms that govern mineral separation and element extraction at the molecular level can be attained. The rapid advancement of nanomechanical techniques, such as Atomic Force Microscopy (AFM) and Surface Force Apparatus (SFA), has facilitated a shift from qualitative characterization to quantitative measurement in surface and interface chemistry studies. Nonetheless, bridging the gap between these test results and practical mineral separation and metal enrichment tests remains a formidable challenge.

This Special Issue aims to provide a comprehensive platform to disseminate cutting-edge research and advance in applying surface and interface chemistry to the fields of mineral separation and element extraction, which cover a wide range of topics, including but not limited to:
● Advanced surface and interface analytical techniques used in mineral separation and element extraction;
● Applications of advanced quantum chemistry and molecular dynamics simulations in mineral separation and element extraction;
● Novel approaches for enhancing the efficiency and selectivity of interfacial processes;
● Computational modeling and experimental methodologies to unravel the complexities of interfacial reactions;
● Other relevant aspects of surface and interface chemistry applied to mineral separation and element extraction, such as surface modification techniques and reagent design.

The Special Issue welcomes original research articles, short communications, and review articles.
keywords: ["Surface and interface chemistry", "mineral separation", "element extraction", "adsorption", "flotation", "leaching", "computational modeling", "quantum chemistry", "molecular dynamics simulation"]

topic: Topic: The Latest Research on Catalyst for Seawater Electrolysis
summary:
Hydrogen production through water electrolysis provides an economically viable and straightforward approach, demonstrating considerable potential for various applications. As freshwater resources become scarcer, the importance of seawater electrolysis grows significantly, given that it accounts for about 97% of the global water supply. Thus, seawater electrolysis stands at the forefront of sustainable hydrogen production, signaling a shift towards cleaner energy practices. The Special Issue explores pivotal advancements in electrocatalysis that are set to enhance the efficiency and output of this process. Central themes include the integration of innovative anodic oxidation reactions and seamless synergy with renewable energy systems, which are critical for optimizing seawater electrolysis and its role in the hydrogen economy. This collection seeks to encapsulate the interdisciplinary research that underpins the scalable and environmentally responsible advancement of seawater electrolysis, marking a new chapter in hydrogen fuel generation.

Key research areas encapsulated in this Special Issue are:
● Electrocatalyst Development: Innovations in materials science are driving the efficiency of hydrogen production, serving as a cornerstone for energy-efficient seawater electrolysis.
● Optimizing Oxygen Evolution Selectivity: Enhancing the oxygen evolution reaction selectivity in seawater electrolysis is essential to counteract the competing chlorine evolution reactions. Developing methods to minimize chlorine reaction interference is crucial for enhancing the efficiency of oxygen evolution and promoting sustainable hydrogen production.
● Anodic Oxidation Innovations: Exploring advancements in anodic oxidation reactions, such as urea oxidation, hydroxymethylfurfural oxidation, hydrazine oxidation, etc., is paramount for boosting the efficiency of electrolysis systems. This targeted innovation is expected to substantially improve the performance and efficacy of electrolytic cells.
● Seawater Electrolysis and Energy Device Integration: Examining the integration of seawater electrolysis with energy devices, particularly in industrial-scale or high-current scenarios, underscores a strategic move towards enhanced energy sustainability. This exploration highlights the effective amalgamation of electrolysis technologies within diverse energy infrastructures, setting the stage for more robust and expansive energy systems.
keywords: ["Seawater electrolysis", "hydrogen production", "electrocatalysis", "oxygen evolution reaction", "chlorine evolution reaction", "anodic oxidation reaction", "system integration"]

source: Journal of Surveillance, Security and Safety
topic: Topic: Security and Privacy Challenges and Solutions for IoT/6G Scenarios
summary:
With the rapid evolution of communication technologies, it is anticipated that 6G will be transformative and revolutionize the evolution of wireless from "connected things" to "connected intelligence". With the robust integration of the Internet of Things (IoT) and Beyond 5G (B5G) or 6G, industries are expected to advance with significant economic growth. However, integrating these emerging technologies necessitates a greater variety of technologies and devices. Some are robust systems with enforced protections, while others manifest as defective components with glaring vulnerabilities. Consequently, security hazards and the infected potential of the connected systems will be integral elements of insecure communication.

Enhancing security protection for the system and deterring possible threats requires diverse security mechanisms with their mindful orchestration. As the use of digital technologies continues to grow, the need for cryptography as a security mechanism grows significantly. Cyber threats, including hacking, data breaches, and identity theft, are becoming more sophisticated and prevalent. It protects against government surveillance and unauthorized access to personal data. Some security mechanisms and topics are IoT authentication, intrusion detection and prevention, reverse engineering, algorithms used in blockchain technology, mobile devices, IoT, and post-quantum cryptography.

In this regard, we require both application/subsystem-specific security solutions and an integration/framework that federates the various security functions. The articles in this Special Issue provide valuable insights into the latest developments and advancements of the state-of-the-art security and privacy in IoT, B5G, and 6G technologies, making it a must-read for researchers, practitioners, and anyone interested in the security of modern technology. We aim to attract diverse researchers, academics, designers, and others to discuss these issues from various perspectives. This Special Issue seeks original contributions not presently under another journal or conference review. Prospective authors are invited to submit articles reporting on novel and significant research contributions, ongoing research projects, experimental results, and recent developments related, but not limited, to the following topics:
● Lightweight cryptographic algorithms and protocols;
● Authentication, authorization, and accounting (AAA) for advanced computing scenarios;
● Threat modeling and risk assessment for IoT environments;
● Secure device-to-device (D2D) communications;
● Blockchain-based services and applications for IoT;
● Ethical and legal issues related to security and privacy in 6G and IoT technologies;
● Security and privacy issues in 6G networks;
● Wireless virtualization and slicing security;
● Post-quantum algorithms for IoT;
● Secure and privacy-preserving algorithms and models for IoT devices;
● Privacy and security in distributed computing environments;
● Quantum computing for advanced applications;
● Attack detection through cryptanalysis;
● Symmetric cryptography for B5G/6G;
● Neural cryptography.
keywords: ["Internet of Things (IoT)", "Beyond 5G (B5G)", "6G", "security issues", "privacy", "solutions"]

topic: Topic: Updates in Underwater Robotics
summary:
The Special Issue "Updates in Underwater Robotics" serves as a platform that brings together diverse perspectives and innovative research in the field of underwater vehicles, drawing contributions from leading researchers all over the world. With the advancement of ocean research and marine resource exploration, underwater vehicles have gained prominence, assuming a pivotal role in various marine missions, including deep-sea resource exploration, ocean observation, environmental monitoring, offshore inspection, surveillance, and reconnaissance. This compilation of articles delves into novel techniques and advancements that shape the future of underwater vehicles. Explored topics encompass innovative and frontier research in control design, autonomous navigation, and cooperative control for underwater robotics, emphasizing sensor fusion techniques, sonar and image target detection technologies, path planning algorithms, and machine learning methods aimed at optimizing underwater exploration and data collection. The research featured in this Special Issue is pivotal for enhancing underwater robotics capabilities and performance across a spectrum of underwater missions, rendering it an invaluable resource for researchers, engineers, and organizations engaged in marine technology and exploration.

This Special Issue endeavors to present the latest developments in the investigation and practical applications of underwater robotics. Its focus is on reporting theoretical and applied research results in control algorithms, optimization techniques, hydrodynamic analysis, target detection, underwater manipulators, and new conceptual designs of underwater robotics, among others. Additionally, it highlights the use of current artificial intelligence techniques and cutting-edge research, such as machine learning, deep learning, SLAM, and fault tolerance control.
keywords: ["Path planning", "tracking control", "underwater manipulators", "new conceptual underwater robotics", "hydrodynamic analysis", "underwater target detection"]

topic: Topic: The Current Status and Development of Nanocatalysis
summary: The advancement of nanocatalysis aims to enhance catalytic efficiency, selectivity, and economic viability by designing well-structured nanocatalysts. This concerted effort seeks to propel the application of catalytic technology across various domains. By evolving nanocatalysis, we not only advance the forefront of basic science but also provide innovative solutions to address numerous global challenges. Its implications stretch far and wide, influencing sustainable development, environmental protection, and human well-being. This Special Issue invites all types of manuscripts, including Research Articles, Mini-reviews/Reviews, Short communications, Highlights, etc.
keywords: ["Novel nanofunctional materials", "environmentally friendly catalysis", "nanomaterials preparation and properties", "molecular catalysis"]

topic: Topic: Advanced Research on Molecular Mechanisms of Cancer Drug Resistance
summary: The tumor-resident microbiota has been linked to cancer drug resistance, and manipulation of this microbiota is a novel strategy for the prevention and treatment of cancer drug resistance. Some microbiota demonstrate antitumor activity, thereby improving the tumor microenvironment, activating immune cells including dendritic cells, helper T cells, natural killer cells, and cytotoxic T cells, and upregulating the secretion of pro-tumor immune cytokines such as interleukin-2 and interferon. Tumor-resident microbiota and specific metabolites play crucial roles in designing anticancer resistance drug and their targeted delivery.
keywords: ["Tumor-resident microbiota", "Immune checkpoint inhibitors", "microbiota metabolites", "anticancer drug design", "drug targeted delivery"]

topic: Topic: Recent Advances in Robotic-Assisted Micro- and Supemicrosurgery
summary:
We are delighted to present the Special Issue on "Recent Advances in Robotic-Assisted Micro- and Supermicrosurgery", which showcases cutting-edge research and innovations at the intersection of robotics and intricate surgical techniques. The field of robotic-assisted surgery has witnessed remarkable developments, revolutionizing microsurgical procedures with enhanced precision, dexterity, and overall surgical outcomes. This Special Issue delves into the latest breakthroughs in micro- and supermicrosurgery facilitated by robotic systems, spotlighting advancements in precision and the ability to address intricate anatomical structures with greater accuracy or in difficult-to-reach anatomical areas within the human body.

Our objective with this Special Issue is to foster a comprehensive understanding of the synergies between robotics and micro- to supermicrosurgical interventions. The contributions in this collection provide invaluable insights into the evolving landscape of robotic-assisted procedures, offering a glimpse into the future of precise, minimally invasive surgical techniques.

We extend our sincere gratitude to the authors for their valuable contributions and invite the readers to explore the forefront of innovation in robotic-assisted micro- and supermicrosurgery.
keywords: ["Robotic microsurgery", "robotic-assisted lymphatic surgery", "robotic-assisted supermicrosurgery", "central lymphatic surgery", "robotic-assisted central lymphatic surgery"]

topic: Topic: Multi-Dimensional and Interdisciplinary Disaster Resilience Monitoring
summary:
It has long been established that the notion of resilience spans four interrelated dimensions: technical, organizational, social, and economic, collectively referred to as the TOSE dimensions. Therefore, the challenge of developing quantitative methods for measuring resilience has been recognized as early as the 2000s, necessitating researchers to break out of the silos of disciplines. In recent years, we have witnessed prolific, inter-, or even trans-disciplinary endeavors toward measuring and assessing the resilience of civil structures and infrastructure systems. However, we argue that in the arena of multi-dimensional system resilience modeling, most research approaches rely on unverified assumptions and lack support for data. Taking the analogy of structural health monitoring (SHM) and its pivotal relevance to and beneficial impact on structural diagnosis and prognosis, performance-based design, and life cycle structures and infrastructure management, we assert that novel monitoring techniques and innovative solutions are imperative for disaster risk mitigation and disaster resilience pertinent to civil structures, infrastructure systems, and communities.

Disaster Resilience Monitoring is a specialized field of study and application that focuses on developing and implementing monitoring strategies and technologies to enhance the resilience quantification and assessment of critical infrastructure, lifeline structures, and communities when disrupted by natural or man-made disasters. New knowledge, methods, and solutions for this field will directly influence the forthcoming resilience-based engineering design, resilience-based life-cycle maintenance and management, and the nexus of system resilience and sustainability. However, we assert that the challenges in developing disaster resilience monitoring are unparalleled compared to the traditional SHM methods, which entails the fusion of broader discipline knowledge (engineering, electronics, computing, geoscience, social and behavioral sciences, and others), sensing and modeling at higher range of scales (global, regional, community, structures/infrastructure, to individual), and processing and understanding of more heterogeneous data modalities (images, language, maps and graphs, time-series, etc.).

This Special Issue is dedicated to exploring cutting-edge monitoring and modeling methodologies and technologies to enhance the disaster resilience of civil infrastructure, critical structures, and communities.  We invite research, review, and case-study articles that address the monitoring and modeling challenges from all phases of disaster resilience, including preparedness, response, and recovery. We especially solicit articles from interdisciplinary researchers or research teams that advance the frontier of Multi-dimensional and Interdisciplinary Disaster Resilience Monitoring. Suggested topics include, but are not limited to:
● New theories and mathematical frameworks for multi-dimensional and interdisciplinary disaster resilience sensing and modeling;
● Geological and climatic disasters and global and regional monitoring, and impact assessment using satellites and unmanned air/ground vehicles or other sensing modalities;
● Novel conjunction of SHM with disaster resilience sensing;
● Innovative sensing solutions in the areas of sensor networks, robotic sensing, Internet of Things, and crowdsourcing/human sensing (including online surveys) and their applications at all phases of civil systems and community resilience;
● Novel crowdsourcing and human computing methods for disaster preparedness, response, and recovery;
● Novel use of sensing and modeling solutions at the social, organizational, and economic dimensions;
Novel data-driven mining, machine learning, generative artificial intelligence (AI), and digital twining methods;
● Nexus of social and behavioral sciences with technical solutions for disaster resilience, including technology-based social surveys, risk perception and communication, and resilience-based decision-making;
● Disaster debris sensing, measurement, and management.
keywords: ["Disaster resilience", "structural resilience", "community resilience", "resilience monitoring", "resilience quantification", "risk perception", "communication", "mitigation", "sensing and modeling", "structural health monitoring", "emergency response", "sustainable and resilient infrastructure"]

topic: Topic: Advance of Mineral Materials for Wastewater Treatment
summary:
Minerals are the fundamental units and key environmental elements of the Earth system, crucial for human survival and development. Developed from raw materials or based on an understanding of mineral principles, mineral materials play an indispensable role in industrial, economic, and social development. In recent years, the environmental and water pollution problems caused by various types of industrial wastewater, such as printing and dyeing, chemicals, electroplating, mining, smelting, petrochemicals, machining, pharmaceuticals, and municipal sources, have become increasingly prominent. Researchers have developed diverse methods such as adsorption, membrane filtration, chemical oxidation, biological treatment, and advanced oxidation processes to efficiently dispose of and treat water pollution. Among them, utilizing mineral resources, such as diatomite, kaolinite, montmorillonite, sepiolite, halloysite, etc.,  along with the secondary resources with huge reserves and low costs, to develop new mineral materials for wastewater treatment through strategies such as compounding, modification, and crystal form regulation, is of great significance for resource efficiency and sustainable development. Additionally, it promotes green, efficient, and clean treatment of industrial wastewater.

The Special Issue of Minerals and Mineral Materials aims to present state-of-the-art developments and reports of mineral materials for wastewater treatment. We have invited contributions from leading groups in the field to offer a balanced view of this discipline. The subareas to be discussed in this Special Issue include:
1. Preparation of non-metallic mineral materials;
2. Preparation of metal-based mineral materials;
3. Mineral-based adsorbent materials;
4. Mineral-based catalytic materials;
5. Mineral-based flocculation materials;
6. Mineral-based filter materials;
7. Mineral-based ion exchange materials;
8. Mineral materials for advanced oxidation processes;
9. Mineral materials for biofilm carriers;
10. Application of minerals in organic wastewater treatment;
11. Application of minerals in heavy metal wastewater treatment;
12. Application of minerals in inorganic anion wastewater treatment;
13. Preparation of materials derived from solid waste resources;
14. Solid waste resources-based materials for wastewater treatment.

This Special Issue welcomes original research articles, short communications, and review articles.
keywords: ["Natural mineral", "solid waste resources", "secondary resources", "mineral materials", "environmental materials", "wastewater treatment", "adsorption", "photocatalysis", "electrocatalysis", "persulfate activation", "flocculation", "structure regulation"]

topic: Topic: The Current Status and Development of Single-Atom Catalysis
summary: The development of single-atom catalysis aims to enhance catalytic efficiency, selectivity, and economic viability by designing well-structured single-atom catalysts. This effort seeks to propel the application of catalytic technology across various domains. The development of single-atom catalysis not only advances the forefront of basic science but also provides innovative solutions to address many global challenges. It has far-reaching implications for sustainable development, environmental protection, and human well-being. This Special Issue welcomes all types of manuscripts, including Research Articles, Mini-reviews/Reviews, Short communications, Highlights, etc.
keywords: ["Single-atom catalysts", "rational design", "structure characterization", "microenvironment", "electrocatalyst", "photocatalyst", "heterogeneous catalyst"]

topic: Topic: Photocatalysts for Solar Fuels and Chemicals Synthesis
summary:
Photocatalysis technology has been regarded as one of the most promising "green" strategies for dealing with the ever-increasing global fossil fuel shortage and related environmental pollution. More importantly, it provides an ideal way for solar energy conversion, storage, and utilization. In this approach, solar fuels and chemicals can be synthesized, especially H2 evolution through photocatalytic water splitting and the production of high-value hydrocarbon fuels via CO2 reduction. Herein, the design and development of highly efficient semiconductor photocatalysts, integrated photocatalytic systems, and novel reaction processes have been at the forefront of this research field, with remarkable progress achieved to date. Therefore, this Special Issue of "Photocatalysts for Solar Fuels and Chemicals Synthesis" aims to gather current scientific advancements, including original research papers, reviews, and commentaries, in this area. Perspectives on the production of solar fuels and chemicals through photocatalysis are also welcome.

The following specific areas to be covered include, but are not limited to:

● Photocatalysts synthesis;

● Photocatalytic water splitting;

● Photocatalytic CO2 reduction;
● Photocatalytic methane conversion;

● Photocatalytic N2 fixation;

● Biomass conversion;

● Fine chemicals production;

● Photocatalysis coupled with thermocatalysis;

● Advanced characterization techniques for photocatalysts.
keywords: ["Photocatalysis", "water splitting", "CO<sub>2</sub> reduction", "solar fuels", "chemicals", "hydrogen", "biomass", "artificial photosynthesis"]

topic: Topic: Electrochemical Technology for Removing Emerging Pollutants from Water
summary:
Dear Colleagues,

Emerging pollutants such as perfluorinated compounds, antibiotics, and personal care products in water/wastewater present a significant challenge due to their high toxicity, stability, and resistance to conventional treatment methods. At present, the control of these emerging pollutants is still in its early stages, making the advanced treatment of such contaminants a crucial and formidable task in water pollution control chemistry. Efficient removal technology for these pollutants using electrochemical methods holds promise in reducing or completely eliminating their potential risks to human health and ecological safety. The fabrication of functional electrode materials and micro-structure regulation of electrodes, the electron transfer mechanism and decomposition mechanism in the electrochemical degradation process of emerging pollutants, and the potential application of electrochemical technology in the deep purification of emerging pollutants in wastewater have received extensive attention from researchers. Investigations into these aspects will lay a foundation for innovative principles and technologies for the electrocatalytic removal of emerging pollutants in water.

In this context, the Guest Editors invite submissions of papers that contribute to the development of high-performance and stable electrodes, optimization of reactor design and process flow, and other related aspects that promote the use of electrochemical technology for emerging pollutants removal in water/wastewater treatment. Original research studies or review articles encompassing the recent findings and advancements in this field are welcomed for this Special Issue.

Dr. Ran Mao
keywords: ["Electrochemical technology", "emerging pollutants", "perfluorinated compounds", "antibiotics", "personal care products", "advanced oxidation process", "electrocatalysts", "electrochemical oxidation/reduction"]

topic: Topic: Reliability and Risk for Engineering Structures under Natural Hazards
summary:
Reliability Assessment and Risk Management, an interdisciplinary domain related to probability mechanics and engineering disciplines, have been consistently applied in recent decades to confront reliability and risk challenges in various areas such as engineering structures, offshore operations, and marine fields. Engineering structures are prone to long-term environmental effects due to exposure to environmental conditions and natural hazards. The evaluation of the reliability and risk of engineering structures exposed to natural hazards has emerged as a global priority. In particular, reliability assessment and risk management, considering uncertainties for such structures and natural hazards, have proven pivotal in addressing these challenges. This Special Issue aims to provide a forum for researchers to present state-of-the-art research findings and future trends in risk and reliability for engineering structures under long-term environmental effects.

The principal topics to be explored include, but are not limited to:
(1) Reliability assessment methods and risk management strategies;

(2) Hazard modeling and random vibrations; 

(3) Reliability-based performance evaluation;

(4) Intelligent risk assessment under uncertainties.
keywords: ["Reliability analysis", "risk assessment", "structural simulation", "hazards modeling", "traffic loads", "random vibrations", "intelligent assessment"]

topic: Topic: Bioplastics and Microplastics: Mechanisms, Toxicity and Future Prospects
summary:
The global plastic crisis has triggered an urgent search for sustainable alternatives as conventional plastics persist in environmental pollution. Bioplastics, derived from renewable resources and distinguished by their biodegradability, have emerged as potential game-changers. However, the story of bioplastics is intricate, with concerns surrounding microplastic formation, potential toxicity, and their true environmental impact casting doubt on their promise. This Special Issue of Water Emerging Contaminants & Nanoplastics (WECN) delves deep into the complex world of bioplastics, shedding light on their mechanisms, toxicity, and future prospects.

We invite researchers and academics across diverse disciplines to submit original manuscripts that provide critical insights into various aspects of bioplastics, including:

● Bioplastics types and mechanisms: Explore the diverse range of bioplastics, from plant-based to bacterial polymers, and delve into their biodegradation pathways and environmental fate;

● Micro-/nanoplastic formation and release: Investigate the mechanisms by which bioplastics break down into microplastics, including fragmentation during use, composting, and wastewater treatment;

● Toxicity assessment: Evaluate the potential toxicological impacts of bioplastics and associated microplastics on human health and environmental ecosystems. This includes analyzing the leaching of chemicals, adsorption of pollutants, and potential effects on organisms;

● Life cycle analysis: Conduct comprehensive life cycle assessments of bioplastics, comparing their environmental footprint with conventional plastics across production, use, and disposal stages;

● Innovation and future directions: Explore cutting-edge advancements in bioplastics research, including bioplastics with enhanced biodegradability, improved functionalities, and strategies for mitigating microplastic release;

● Policy and economic considerations: Analyze the economic viability and policy frameworks needed to facilitate the widespread adoption and sustainable management of bioplastics.

By bringing together diverse perspectives and cutting-edge research, this Special Issue aims to address the critical questions surrounding bioplastics and inform their responsible development and implementation. A comprehensive understanding of their mechanisms, potential risks, and promising future directions is crucial for navigating the complex path towards a plastic-free future.

We encourage researchers and academics with expertise in bioplastics, biodegradation, microplastics, environmental toxicology, life cycle analysis, and related fields to submit their original manuscripts for consideration. We anticipate insightful contributions that will advance our understanding of bioplastics and shape their trajectory towards a truly sustainable future.
keywords: ["Bioplastics", "microplastics", "nanoplastics", "emerging contamination", "characterization", "toxicity"]

topic: Topic: Intelligent Data Process and Transmission for Wireless Communications
summary:
Intelligent techniques, e.g., deep learning (DL) and deep reinforcement learning (DRL), originally from computer science, are now making their presence in wireless communications. Initially used in upper layers of wireless communications, such as routing optimization and cognitive radio deployment, these techniques are gradually extending their reach into the physical layer.

This Special Issue centers on integrating intelligent techniques into wireless communications, aiming to infuse greater intelligence into future communication systems. We welcome the submission of original and high-quality technical or survey articles that delve into DL/DRL techniques and their applications in wireless communications and signal processing. Submissions should be previously unpublished. The topics covered in this Special Issue include, but are not limited to:
● mm-Wave, sub-THz, and THz radio technologies;
● Applications of wireless communications in Industry 4.0 scenarios;
● Low-latency and high-reliability wireless communications;
● Intelligent modulation techniques;
● Machine learning for coding and decoding;
● DRL techniques for multi-access and NOMA channels;
● Green communication and computing for Beyond 5G (B5G) networks;
● Energy-aware distributed wireless devices;
● Machine learning for MIMO systems;
● Intelligent unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) communications and processing.
keywords: ["Wireless communications", "deep learning (DL)", "machine learning", "physical layer", "intelligent modulation techniques", "MIMO systems", "beyond 5G (B5G) networks", "intelligent UAV communications"]

source: Journal of Surveillance, Security and Safety
topic: Topic: Cybersecurity in Vehicular Networks: Securing Future Intelligent Transportation Systems
summary:
The speedy aggrandizement in wireless communication technologies, concurrent with immediate measure requisites to improve road safety, has expedited considerable development in the Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS). ITS came into existence with a strong perspective to provide end-to-end, better, worthier, safer, more efficient, and much more improved transport services for both on-road and off-road scenarios. Intelligent communication technologies, such as trip guiding smartphone applications (that direct you how much to walk before you take the next bus or train, where to get down, etc.) and the latest safety-oriented vehicle systems (such as Anti-Lock Braking systems, air bags, Navigation systems, etc.), are all part of the ITS. With increasing congestion leading to increased accidents, the government authorities and automobile manufacturers have realized the need to develop new sophisticated schemes for in-vehicular capabilities. Consequently, this has resulted in the formation of Vehicular communication networks termed ‘Vehicular Ad hoc Networks’ (VANETs). VANETs are, therefore, an emerging promising technology of the ITS due to their potential benefits for travel planning, notifying road hazards, cautioning emergency scenarios and alleviating congestion, provisioning parking facilities, and addressing environmental predicaments. Hence, security is the foremost necessity in VANETs.

This Special Issue addresses all aspects related to cybersecurity measures in ITS and associated fields. Specifically, it covers the following topics:
● Authentication schemes in ITS;
● Security issues in designing real-time protocols;
● Privacy-preserving algorithms;
● Trust issues in ITS;
● VANETs security;
● Machine Learning and its applications in ITS;
● End-to-end security in ITS;
● Blockchain and smart contract uses in ITS;
● Key distribution algorithms;
● Real-time applications.
keywords: ["Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS)", "Vehicular Ad hoc Networks (VANETs)", "cybersecurity", "privacy", "security issues"]

source: Extracellular Vesicles and Circulating Nucleic Acids
topic: Topic: Precision Targeting and Blood-Brain Barrier Penetration of Extracellular Vesicles
summary:
Precision targeting includes the development of delivery systems that specifically target desired cells or tissues, minimizing off-target effects and improving the overall efficacy of therapeutic interventions. This approach often utilizes various targeting ligands or surface modifications to guide drug-loaded vesicles to their intended destination with high specificity. The blood-brain barrier (BBB) is a highly selective barrier that protects the brain from circulating toxins and pathogens but poses a challenge for drug delivery to the central nervous system (CNS). Overcoming the BBB is crucial for the therapy of neurological diseases. Extracellular vesicles (EVs), such as exosomes, have gained attention as potential carriers for drug delivery due to their natural ability to transport cargo between cells. Strategies to enhance BBB penetration involve optimizing the properties of EVs or incorporating specific modifications that facilitate their transport across the BBB. Precision targeting and BBB penetration in the context of EVs will provide promising avenues for the development of more effective and targeted therapies, particularly in the area of neurodegenerative disorders and other diseases affecting the CNS.

Topic：
1. Neurological Disorders and Treatment: Examine specific neurological disorders such as Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, brain tumors, stroke, traumatic brain injury, Encephalitis, etc., and explore how precision targeting and BBB penetration strategies can be tailored for effective therapeutic interventions.
2. Nanotechnology in Drug Delivery: Delve into the nanotechnological aspects of drug delivery, highlighting nanoparticles, liposomes, and other nanocarriers that can aid in overcoming biological barriers, such as the BBB, for precise drug delivery.
3. Molecular Engineering for EVs: Explore the molecular modifications and engineering techniques applied to EVs to improve their stability, cargo-loading capacity, and ability to traverse biological barriers, especially the BBB.
4. Blood-Brain Barrier Challenges: Discuss the unique challenges posed by the BBB in drug delivery and how these challenges impact the treatment of neurological disorders.
5. Targeted Drug Delivery Systems: Explore various strategies for precision targeting, including ligand-based approaches, antibody targeting, and other surface modifications, to enhance the specificity of drug-loaded vesicles.
6. Clinical Applications and Trials: Review current and potential clinical applications of precision-targeted drug delivery systems using EVs, including ongoing trials and research advancements.
7. Biomarkers for Precision Targeting: Discuss the identification and utilization of biomarkers that can aid in the precision targeting of EVs to specific cells or tissues.
8. Biological Barriers Beyond the BBB: Consider other biological barriers, such as the blood-cerebrospinal fluid barrier, and strategies to overcome these barriers for improved drug delivery.
9. Ethical and Safety Considerations: Address ethical considerations and safety aspects related to the use of precision-targeted drug delivery systems, particularly when applied to the CNS.
keywords: ["Extracellular vesicles", "exosomes", "drug delivery", "blood-brain barrier", "neurological disorders", "nanoparticles", "nanotechnology", "molecular engineering", "precision medicine", "biomarkers"]

topic: Topic: The Development of Advanced Materials and Catalysis
summary:
The field of advanced materials and catalysis stands at the forefront of scientific and technological innovation, driving progress in diverse industries ranging from energy to healthcare. This dynamic and multidisciplinary domain encompasses the design, synthesis, and application of materials with tailored properties, alongside the exploration of catalytic processes that underpin numerous chemical transformations.

Recently, the demand for materials with enhanced functionalities has surged, spurred by the quest for sustainable solutions and unprecedented technological challenges. Advanced materials, characterized by unique structural features at the nanoscale, offer a wealth of opportunities to revolutionize key sectors. From nanomaterials with superior mechanical properties to catalysts with enhanced selectivity, these materials hold the promise of transforming the landscape of modern applications.

Catalysis, as an integral component of this field, plays a pivotal role in accelerating chemical reactions, enabling more sustainable processes, and facilitating the production of valuable compounds. The rational design of catalysts, often at the atomic or molecular level, opens avenues for unprecedented selectivity and efficiency in various transformations. The synergy between advanced materials and catalysis has emerged as a focal point, with researchers striving to develop innovative materials that serve as efficient and selective catalyst supports.

This Special Issue aims to provide a platform for researchers to share their latest findings, methodologies, and insights in the development of advanced materials and catalysis. From fundamental studies elucidating the intricate mechanisms governing catalytic processes to the application of novel materials in real-world scenarios, the contributions within this issue are poised to shape the trajectory of these intertwined fields. As we delve into the intricate world of advanced materials and catalysis, we anticipate uncovering transformative discoveries that will propel us toward a more sustainable and technologically advanced future.
keywords: ["Nanomaterials", "catalytic processes", "renewable energy", "materials design"]

topic: Topic: Advancements in Hydrogen Production Technologies and Materials
summary:
As an ideal secondary energy source with high energy density, hydrogen energy is considered an ideal energy carrier. In the context of carbon neutrality, hydrogen energy is the category of new energy that is most closely integrated with the existing business of the oil and gas industry, and it is also one of the best ways to help the oil and gas industry achieve carbon peak and carbon neutrality at an early date. However, with the decline of fossil fuel production and the deepening of the concept of sustainable development, the hydrogen market of the Hydrogen Club will form a diversified hydrogen supply pattern, with renewable energy (Solar photocatalytic hydrogen production, thermochemical hydrogen production, solar photoelectrocatalytic hydrogen production, water electrolysis, etc.) as the mainstay, supplemented by hydrogen from coal, biomass, and so on. Therefore, it is urgent to actively develop and explore Advanced Hydrogen Production Technologies and Materials.

Hydrogen is the most important clean and renewable energy. Hydrogen Production Technologies and Materials have a very broad application prospect in solving energy and environmental challenges.

Specific areas to be covered include, but are not limited to:
● Advancements in hydrogen production;
● Advanced hydrogen technologies;
● Hydrogen-related materials and technologies;
● Design and fabrication of advanced hydrogen-related materials.
keywords: ["Advancements in hydrogen production", "advanced hydrogen technologies", "hydrogen materials and technologies", "design and fabrication of advanced materials"]

topic: Topic: Innovative Technologies for Green and Sustainable Manufacturing
summary:
Increasingly serious environmental pollution and gradually depleted natural resources have become major issues for mankind. Thus, it is urgent to develop innovative materials, processes, and technologies to enhance both economic and environmental benefits so as to achieve green manufacturing and sustainable development by minimizing adverse environmental impacts while conserving energy and natural resources. Firstly, in order to improve sustainability, innovative materials, such as biodegradable materials, lightweight materials, recyclable materials, artificial materials, metamaterials, and so on, should be introduced. Secondly, innovative processing approaches, such as ultrafast laser direct writing, additive manufacturing, and virtual manufacturing, which can reduce material consumption, waste generation, and energy consumption, are also key factors in maintaining sustainable development in green manufacturing. Finally, innovative technologies, such as big data analysis, digital twin, augmented reality, industrial internet of things, and artificial intelligence, can realize real-time monitoring and prediction of the production process. These innovative technologies can be applied in the manufacturing to optimize the production process and reduce unnecessary energy consumption and material waste.

The following topics will be discussed in this Special Issue:
● Biodegradable materials;
● Lightweight materials;
● Recyclable materials;
● Artificial materials;
● Metamaterials;
● Ultrafast laser direct writing;
● Additive manufacturing;
● Virtual manufacturing;
● Big data analysis;
● Digital twin;
● Augmented reality;
● Industrial internet of things;
● Artificial intelligence.
keywords: ["Green manufacturing", "sustainable manufacturing", "innovative materials", "innovative processes", "innovative technologies"]

topic: Topic: Revealing the Relationship Between Metabolic Dysfunction and Neurodegenerative Diseases
summary:
Neurodegenerative diseases such as Alzheimer's disease (AD), Parkinson's disease (PD), Huntington's disease, and multiple sclerosis are prevalent and debilitating neurological disorders. Recent studies have revealed the significance of metabolic changes in neurons and glia in the pathogenesis of these diseases. For example, abnormal glucose metabolism, iron metabolism and lipid metabolism contribute to neuronal damage in AD and PD. However, the relationship between metabolic dysfunction and neurodegenerative diseases is complex and multifaceted. Further research is imperative to elucidate the underlying mechanisms and develop effective therapeutic interventions targeting metabolic pathways in neurodegenerative diseases.

In this Research Topic, we aim to compile Original Research and Reviews to provide deeper insights into the cellular and molecular mechanisms of metabolic dysfunction in neurodegenerative disorders and therapeutic strategies targeting metabolic pathways for the treatment of these conditions.
keywords: ["Neurodegenerative disease", "Iron metabolism", "glucose metabolism", "lipid metabolism", "metabolic dysfunction", "Parkinson’s disease", "Alzheimer's disease"]

topic: Topic: Design of Polymer and Its Composites Driven by Data and Domain Knowledge
summary:
The Journal of Materials Informatics is delighted to announce a forthcoming Special Issue dedicated to the cutting-edge domain of "Design of Polymer and its Composites driven by data and domain knowledge". This issue is particularly timely, reflecting the pivotal role that data-centric approaches, including artificial intelligence, machine learning, data science, and big data analytics, are playing in the revolution of polymer science and engineering.

This thematic issue seeks to showcase the forefront of research in the field of polymers and their composites, explored through the lens of data-driven and domain knowledge-driven methodologies. The issue will encompass a broad spectrum of topics, including but not limited to:
1. Advanced Algorithms and Computational Models: Development of innovative data-driven algorithms and computational models specifically tailored for the study and design of polymers and their composites.
2. Data-driven Design and Structural Architecture: Exploration of how data analytics can influence the design and structural architecture of polymers for enhanced performance.
3. Physicochemical Properties Analysis: In-depth data-driven and knowledge-driven analyses of the diverse physicochemical properties of polymers and composites, contributing to a deeper understanding and novel applications.
4. Molecular-level Understanding: Integrating domain knowledge into data-centric approaches to decipher the growth, formation mechanisms, and dynamic evolution of polymers at the molecular scale.
5. Functional Applications: Investigation of polymer-based functional devices, their development, and applications in various sectors, guided by data and domain knowledge insights.
6. Sustainable Development Applications: Studies focusing on the role of polymers in energy, environmental solutions, and sustainable development, underpinned by data-driven methodologies.
7. Experimental Techniques and Functionalization: Reports on the experimental preparation, characterization, and functionalization of polymers and composites, enhanced through data-driven approaches.
keywords: ["Advanced algorithms and computational models", "data-driven design and structural architecture", "physicochemical properties analysis", "molecular-level understanding", "functional applications", "sustainable development applications", "experimental techniques and functionalization"]

topic: Topic: AI-Assisted Robotic Cardiac Surgery: A Vision of the Future
summary: Cardiovascular disease remains the leading cause of mortality worldwide, claiming over 17 million deaths annually. Performing complex heart surgeries safely and effectively presents immense challenges. However, the convergence of robotic surgical systems with artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning has the potential to revolutionize cardiovascular treatment and drastically improve patient outcomes. This Special Issue aims to analyze the evolution of AI-assisted robotics transforming cardiovascular surgery, discuss the latest technological advancements, real-world implementation, benefits demonstrated, and predict what the future may hold for this crucial field of cardiovascular surgery.
keywords: ["Robotic Cardiac surgery", "cardiovascular disease", "Artificial Intelligence"]

topic: Topic: Biomass for Energy Storage
summary:
Due to the natural sustainability of biomass energy, leveraging it through various technologies provides an alternative carbon-negative strategy to contribute to carbon neutrality. With the great development of nanomaterials, biotechnology, and processing regulation, biomass utilization is gaining extensive attention. Combining sustainable technologies such as photocatalysis, electrocatalysis, biocatalysis, etc. with biomass conversion is shedding new light on biomass utilization, biofuels production, hydrogen generation, and environmental remediation. It is becoming clear that the advancements in catalytic materials, reaction processing regulations, mechanical investigations, economic feasibility studies, and industrial applications will mainly facilitate the biomass utilization for energy storage.

Specific areas to be covered include, but are not limited to:

● Sustainable photo(electro)catalysis for biomass utilization/pretreatment;
● Biomass gasification for biofuels and/or chemicals;

● Processing investigations for industrialization;

● Biomass treatment for functional materials;

● Hydrogen production and CO2 reduction coupled with biomass conversion.
