Recent advances in genomics have significantly transformed our understanding of the pathophysiology, diagnosis, and treatment of HCM. In the genomic era, novel insights into the genetic basis of the disease, combined with innovations in diagnostic technologies and personalized therapeutic approaches, offer new possibilities for improved patient management and outcomes.

This Special Issue seeks to explore the latest developments in the field of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, with a particular focus on the integration of genomic data into clinical practice. We welcome original research, reviews, and clinical case studies that address topics including, but not limited to:
● The role of genetic mutations in the onset and progression of HCM;
● Novel therapeutic strategies, including gene-based therapies and pharmacologic innovations;
● Insights from large-scale genomic and phenotypic studies;
● Ethical considerations and challenges in genetic screening and counseling.

This Special Issue aims to provide a comprehensive overview of how genomic advancements are reshaping the landscape of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, offering new perspectives on diagnosis and treatment. We encourage submissions from researchers, clinicians, and scholars working at the intersection of cardiology, genetics, and molecular medicine.
keywords: ["Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy", "HCM", "genomic", "cardiovascular disorders"]

topic: Topic: The Potential of Biochar in Water Treatment: Mechanisms and Applications for Organic Pollutant Removal
summary:
Biochar is a porous carbon-rich material prepared by pyrolysis or hydrothermal carbonization of biomass. In recent years, biochar has attracted increasing attention worldwide due to its excellent performance in carbon sequestration, environmental remediation, and soil improvement. Additionally, biochar has exhibited great potential in the removal of water pollutants. However, certain limitations - such as stability issues and potential biological toxicity - still pose challenges to its application in water treatment, requiring further investigation refinement. 

Scope of the Special Issue:

This Special Issue aims to provide a comprehensive overview of the latest developments and innovations in biochar's use for organic pollutant removal in water treatment. Topics of interest include, but are not limited to:

● Novel conversion technologies of biochar for water treatment;

● Biochar for nutrient transformation in water treatment; 

● Microbial responses to biochar in water treatment;

● Biochar-associated free radicals for organic pollutant removal;

● Stability and risk assessment of biochar; 

● Advances in biochar application for water treatment.
keywords: ["Biochar", "organic pollutant removal", "adsorption", "free radicals", "emerging pollutants", "biological toxicity", "carbon sequestration"]

topic: Topic: Mechanisms of Cellular Quality Control and Cell Death in the Heart
summary: In response to cardiac stress, cellular quality control processes, such as autophagy, are activated to clear damaged proteins and organelles. However, excessive activation of cellular quality control mechanisms, or inability to clear cellular debris may lead to activation of cell death mechanisms in the heart. Since the human heart has limited capacity for cardiac myocyte regeneration after injury, dysregulation of these mechanisms may lead to heart disease, the leading cause of death worldwide. This Special Issue will include original research articles and reviews highlighting new advancements in the area of cellular quality control mechanisms and cell death in the heart. We invite submissions of novel, exciting research on wide-ranging topics in cellular quality control mechanisms of cardiovascular health and disease, including autophagy, cell death, mitochondrial function, apoptosis, necrosis, cardiac injury, doxorubicin-induced cardiotoxicity, women's heart health, circadian biology, stem cells, and novel threptic approaches to mitigate cell death in the heart.
keywords: ["Cellular quality control", "autophagy", "cell death", "mitochondria", "apoptosis", "doxorubicin", "women’s heart health", "circadian biology"]

topic: Topic: Integrated Wearable Devices for Energy Management and Health Monitoring
summary:
Integrated wearable devices hold immense potential for healthcare innovation, offering a seamless way to monitor health indicators in continuous, real-time monitoring of physiological signals and chemical biomarkers. These devices combine advanced biosensing technologies with energy supplying systems, facilitating extended personalized health monitoring. Despite significant progress in this field, challenges remain in efficient energy harvesting and storage, miniaturization, integration, and achieving the required sensor accuracy and durability of wearable systems.

This Special Issue focuses on the latest innovations in wearable devices, addressing hurdles such as reliable energy harvesting, wireless data transmission, and device adaptability. Topics of interest include advances in flexible materials, self-powered sensors, and integrated energy storage systems that enhance both the sensing or energy management functionality and user comfort of these devices. By exploring cutting-edge research, this issue aims to showcase how integrated wearable technology drives the future of healthcare and energy management, making continuous, autonomous health monitoring a reality.

The topics covered include, but are not limited to:
● Innovative sensor designs for continuous health monitoring;
● Advances in energy harvesting and storage for self-powered wearables;
● Flexible and stretchable materials for wearable applications;
● Integrated systems for wireless data transmission and on-body analytics;
● Biocompatible and biodegradable materials for long-term health monitoring;
● Miniaturization techniques for compact and multifunctional wearable devices;
● Wearable biosensors for disease management and diagnostics;
● Multi-module wearable sensing platform for biochemical biomarkers and physiological signals.
keywords: ["Wearable sensors", "energy harvesting", "flexible battery", "integrated system", "soft materials"]

topic: Topic: Condition Monitoring, Diagnosis and Predictive Maintenance of Complex Engineering Equipment
summary:
With the continuous progress of modern science and technology, complex engineering equipment (e.g., wind turbines, aero-engine, crane machinery, railway traffic equipment) is rapidly evolving toward increased automation, intelligence, and efficiency. Condition monitoring, diagnosis and predictive maintenance of engineering equipment can be summarized as a comprehensive process. It involves using scientific methods for real-time monitoring of equipment operation status, employing advanced signal processing and fault diagnosis technology to accurately identify abnormal parts, deterioration levels, and development trends, and developing targeted and reasonable preventive and maintenance management strategies. Additionally, it is not only a key measure to avoid downtime accidents, reduce production risks, and improve equipment reliability, but also an effective guarantee to help enterprises operate efficiently, optimize industrial production efficiency, and reduce economic losses. It is a key development area that has attracted special attention from numerous domestic and foreign research institutions, including universities, enterprises, and research institutes. Against this backdrop, this Special Issue has been organized to present the latest advancements in condition monitoring, diagnosis, and predictive maintenance of complex engineering equipment in various fields such as machinery, electrical, civil engineering, and chemical engineering.

This Special Issue aims to collect the latest research findings in the condition monitoring, diagnosis, and predictive maintenance of engineering equipment, while promoting the development of relevant theoretical methods and practical engineering applications in this field. Main topics include (but are not limited to):
1. System dynamics modeling;
2. Wired/wireless sensors and network communication technology;
3. Analysis and processing methods for non-stationary signals;
4. Physical model-based/knowledge-based/data-driven fault diagnosis and predictive maintenance techniques;
5. Software and hardware systems and automatic control technologies for condition monitoring and fault diagnosis of complex engineering equipment.
keywords: ["Dynamics modeling", "digital twin", "sensor technology", "network communication technology", "signal processing", "feature extraction", "fault diagnosis", "pattern recognition", "life prediction", "control strategy", "edge computing", "federated learning", "deep learning", "transfer learning", "reinforcement learning; meta-learning; graph learning"]

topic: Topic: Advances in Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Systems
summary:
The field of robotics is undergoing a transformative phase, driven by rapid advancements in artificial intelligence, particularly in reinforcement learning. Reinforcement learning has emerged as a pivotal approach for endowing robots with the ability to learn complex behaviors through interactions with their environment. This paradigm shift enables robots to perform tasks with increased autonomy and adaptability across diverse and dynamic settings.

This Special Issue focuses on the latest advancements in reinforcement learning applied to robotic systems. We aim to compile state-of-the-art research that highlights both theoretical innovations and practical applications, offering a comprehensive view of the current trends and future directions in this vibrant field.

This Special Issue invites high-quality, original research articles, review papers, and case studies that focus on, but are not limited to, the following topics:
● Deep reinforcement learning and its applications in robotics;
● Multi-agent reinforcement learning for collaborative robotic systems;
● Hierarchical reinforcement learning methods for complex task decomposition;
● Sim-to-real transfer techniques for reinforcement learning in robotics;
● Applications of reinforcement learning in industrial automation;
● Reinforcement learning for humanoid and legged robots;
● Safe and ethical reinforcement learning approaches in robotic decision-making;
● Integration of reinforcement learning with other artificial intelligence paradigms in robotics;
● Real-time learning and adaptation in dynamic environments;
● Benchmarking and evaluation methodologies for reinforcement learning-based robotic systems.
keywords: ["Deep learning", "reinforcement learning", "robotic systems", "multi-agent", "autonomy and adaptability"]

topic: Topic: Data-Driven Intelligent Systems: Challenges and Innovations
summary:
As the volume of data generated continues to surge, data-driven intelligent systems have become essential for leveraging this information to drive innovation and enhance decision-making across various sectors. These systems utilize advanced algorithms and machine learning techniques to extract meaningful insights from vast datasets, facilitating smarter applications in fields such as intelligent vehicles, smart recommender systems, smart cities, intelligent healthcare systems, and intelligent agricultural systems. However, the rapid evolution of data-driven technologies also presents significant challenges, including issues related to data quality, privacy, security, and algorithmic bias. The increasing complexity of data sources and the demand for real-time processing further complicate the development and deployment of these intelligent systems.

This Special Issue aims to explore the latest advancements, challenges, and innovative solutions in the realm of data-driven intelligent systems. We seek to provide a comprehensive understanding of current trends and future directions in this dynamic field. Topics of interest include, but are not limited to:
● Machine Learning and Deep Learning Techniques;
● Data Quality Assessment and Improvement;
● Privacy-Preserving Data Mining;
● Algorithmic Fairness and Bias Mitigation;
● Real-Time Data Processing and Analytics;
● Data Integration and Fusion Techniques;
● Edge Computing for Data-Driven Applications;
● Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI);
● Cooperation and control of intelligent multi-robotic systems;
● Network and communication security of intelligent vehicles;
● Applications of Intelligent Systems in Various Domains.

Academics, research students, and professionals are invited to submit their original work. Extended conference papers with at least 50% new content are also welcome.
keywords: ["Data-driven systems", "machine learning", "explainable artificial intelligence (XAI)"]

source: Journal of Translational Genetics and Genomics
topic: Topic: Combining Drug Repurposing with non-coding RNAs Targeting in Cancer
summary:
This Special Issue highlights innovative strategies that integrate drug repurposing with non-coding RNA (ncRNA) targeting to enhance cancer therapies, presenting a cost-effective and time-efficient approach to discovering new therapeutic uses for existing drugs. Non-coding RNAs, including microRNAs and long non-coding RNAs, have emerged as key regulators of gene expression and are implicated in cancer development and progression. By targeting ncRNAs, researchers can modulate cancer-related pathways, enhancing the therapeutic effects of repurposed drugs.

We invite original research articles, reviews, and clinical studies that explore:

● The role of non-coding RNAs in cancer progression and therapy resistance;

● Innovative drug repurposing strategies that incorporate non-coding RNA (ncRNA) targeting, which involves identifying new therapeutic uses for existing drugs, thereby offering a cost-effective and time-efficient approach to enhancing cancer treatment;

● Preclinical and clinical evaluations of ncRNA-targeting therapies combined with repurposed drugs;

● Insights into the molecular mechanisms driving the success of these combined approaches.

This Special Issue aims to provide a comprehensive overview of how the intersection of drug repurposing and ncRNA research can transform cancer treatment, offering new hope for overcoming therapy resistance and improving patient outcomes.
keywords: ["Non-coding RNA (ncRNA)", "cancer", "therapy resistance"]

topic: Topic: 3D/4D Bioprinting: Innovation, Challenges, and Applications
summary: As an advanced manufacturing method, 3D/4D bioprinting is advancing biomedical engineering, tissue engineering, and personalized medicine. By precisely layering cells, biomaterials, and bioactive factors, 3D bioprinting offers unprecedented opportunities for customizing complex tissues and organs. Additionally, 4D bioprinting introduces the dimension of time, enabling the fabrication of smart structures with adaptive and dynamic response capabilities, thus opening up diverse applications in regenerative medicine, drug delivery, and soft robotics. However, despite its promising potential, 3D/4D bioprinting still faces numerous challenges, such as limitations in material selection, the need for higher printing precision, ensuring cell viability, fabricating multicellular and hierarchical structures, and maintaining long-term structural stability. This Special Issue aims to showcase leading global research, highlighting innovative applications and challenges of 3D/4D bioprinting in personalized medicine while exploring future directions for its development.
keywords: ["3D/4D bioprinting", "tissue engineering", "regenerative medicine", "adaptive materials", "personalized medicine"]

topic: Topic: Innovations in Aerogels: From Synthesis to Applications
summary: Aerogels, renowned for their ultra-low density, high porosity, and remarkable thermal insulation properties, represent a transformative class of materials with diverse applications. Recent innovations in their synthesis and functionalization have unlocked new potential across various industries, including energy storage, environmental remediation, biomedicine, etc. This Special Issue seeks to highlight cutting-edge research that explores novel fabrication techniques, structural enhancements, and the integration of aerogels into advanced technological applications. By gathering contributions from leading researchers, it will provide a comprehensive view of the latest trends and future prospects in aerogel science.
keywords: ["Aerogels", "energy storage", "environmental remediation", "thermal insulation", "biomedical applications", "emerging technologies"]

source: Journal of Translational Genetics and Genomics
topic: Topic: Exploring Clinical Phenotypes and Genotypes of Sepsis
summary:
Sepsis, a life-threatening condition resulting from an intense response to infection, can lead to severe organ failure and death. With diverse clinical phenotypes, recent research underscores the influence of genetics on susceptibility, disease progression, and treatment response. These insights offer promising avenues for personalized therapies in sepsis care.

This Special Issue invites research articles, reviews, and clinical studies on:

●  Genetic markers associated with different sepsis phenotypes;

●  How genotypes affect sepsis manifestations;

●  Emerging genotype-based therapeutic strategies;

●  Ethical and clinical implications of genetic data in sepsis management.

By integrating genetics and clinical insights, this issue aims to advance personalized approaches and deepen our understanding of the genotype-phenotype relationship in sepsis.
keywords: ["Sepsis", "genetic marker", "clinical phenotypes", "genotype-phenotype relationship"]

topic: Topic: Photocatalysts for Solar Energy Conversion: Recent Advances and Applications
summary:
In recent decades, photocatalytic materials have demonstrated substantial research value in various applications, including water splitting, carbon dioxide reduction, hydrogen generation, photooxidation, pollutant degradation, and organic conversion. Despite significant advancements, the application of photocatalysis in the selective synthesis of target compounds, photocatalytic organic conversion, and the efficiency of hydrogen generation from water remains suboptimal. This ongoing challenge continues to attract global interest from chemists and materials scientists. This Special Issue aims to compile high-quality original research articles, commentaries, and viewpoints, with a particular emphasis on the application of photocatalysts in photoinduced organic conversion, water splitting, and environmental remediation.

Topics of interest include, but are not limited to:

● Innovative photocatalyst design, encompassing nanostructured materials, heterojunctions, defect engineering, and advanced photocatalytic composites;

● Photocatalysis for solar fuel production, C1 chemistry, artificial nitrogen fixation, and other solar-to-chemical energy conversion processes;

● Investigations into multifunctional assisted photocatalysis, including piezoelectric photocatalysis, photothermal catalysis, and magnetism, to explore their synergistic effects on performance enhancement and conduct in-depth analyses of the underlying mechanisms;

● Research on photocatalysis to drive advanced oxidation processes for air and water purification, as well as the conversion of waste into valuable resources.
keywords: ["Photocatalyst", "solar fuels", "clean energy", "carbon conversion", "organic synthesis", "pollutant degradation", "CO<sub>2</sub> reduction", "water splitting", "photoredox"]

source: Journal of Translational Genetics and Genomics
topic: Topic: Molecular Genetics and Pathophysiology of Cystic Kidney Diseases
summary:
This Special Issue focuses on the molecular genetics and pathophysiology underlying cystic kidney diseases, characterized by kidney cyst formation that leads to renal dysfunction and progressive kidney failure. These conditions often arise from genetic mutations that alter the morphology and function of primary cilia.

Recent advancements in genomic technologies, such as next-generation sequencing and CRISPR gene editing, have greatly improved our understanding of the genetic factors involved in cystic kidney diseases. This expanding knowledge is vital for developing targeted therapies and improving patient management.

We invite submissions of original research articles, reviews, and clinical studies that explore:

● Genetic mutations associated with cystic kidney diseases;

● Molecular pathways involved in cyst formation and kidney dysfunction;

● Innovative therapeutic approaches targeting these genetic defects;

● Clinical implications of genetic findings for patient outcomes.

By bringing together the latest research in genetics and therapy, this Special Issue aims to advance our understanding and the treatment of cystic kidney diseases.
keywords: ["Cystic kidney diseases", "genetic mutations", "genomic technologies", "next-generation sequencing", "CRISPR gene editing"]

topic: Topic: Flexible Bio-interface Sensing Devices
summary:
With the rapid development of flexible electronics technology, flexible bio-interface sensing devices are transforming the application landscape in the fields of biomedicine, wearable devices, and brain-machine interfaces. These devices not only possess high flexibility, stretchability, and biocompatibility but also achieve seamless integration with the human body and biological tissues. Whether in healthcare monitoring, motion tracking, or applications in neuroscience and rehabilitation, flexible bio-interface sensors demonstrate immense potential.

This Special Issue aims to gather the latest research findings on flexible sensors and bio-interface devices, exploring key challenges from materials design and manufacturing technologies to application innovations. We encourage researchers to share their breakthroughs in areas such as soft materials, biosignal detection, and interface integration.
keywords: ["Flexible electronics", "bio-interface sensors", "wearable devices", "brain-machine interface", "biocompatibility", "integrated sensor"]

source: Journal of Cancer Metastasis and Treatment
topic: Topic: Complications and Side Effects in Cancer Treatment: Pathophysiology and Management
summary:
This Special Issue aims to deepen our understanding of the complications and side effects associated with cancer treatments, which is crucial for enhancing patient quality of life and ensuring treatment adherence. Cancer itself often leads to a myriad of complications, and effectively preventing and managing these issues is crucial in clinical practice. Furthermore, cancer therapies—such as chemotherapy, radiotherapy, immunotherapy, and targeted treatments—frequently result in adverse effects that can impact multiple organ systems and ultimately compromise patient outcomes.

Understanding the pathophysiology behind these complications and side effects is key to developing effective management strategies that alleviate patient discomfort and minimize long-term complications.

We invite research articles and reviews that explore topics, including but not limited to:

● Mechanisms Underlying Treatment-Related Toxicities: Investigate the biological pathways and mechanisms that cause toxicities across various organ systems.

● Novel Biomarkers for Predicting or Monitoring Side Effects: Explore innovative biomarkers for predicting or monitoring treatment-related side effects, enabling personalized care.

● Approaches for Mitigating Side Effects: Discuss strategies and interventions to reduce side effects in diverse cancer populations, considering factors such as age, gender, and comorbidities.

● Patient-Centered Management Strategies: Review advancements in supportive care innovations and patient-centered approaches to managing side effects.

● Long-Term Side Effects and Survivorship Care: Analyze the long-term consequences of cancer treatments and their implications for survivorship care, including monitoring and rehabilitation strategies.

● Coping Strategies for Cancer-Related Complications: Examine psychological mechanisms and coping strategies employed by patients facing cancer-related complications.

● Emerging Technologies and Strategies: Investigate new technologies and strategies designed to minimize potential risks associated with cancer treatments, focusing on safety and efficacy.

● Risks of New Therapeutics: Assess the potential risks and side effect profiles of novel therapies and drugs, along with their management strategies.

By bringing together the latest research, this Special Issue aims to advance the understanding of cancer-related complications and treatment-associated side effects while promoting personalized, tolerable approaches to cancer care.
keywords: ["Cancer treatment", "side effects", "chemotherapy", "radiotherapy", "immunotherapy", "targeted treatment"]

source: Journal of Translational Genetics and Genomics
topic: Topic: Long non-coding RNAs in the Epigenetic Regulation of Cardiac Diseases
summary:
Long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) have emerged as pivotal regulators of gene expression through epigenetic pathways, significantly impacting cardiovascular health. Unlike protein-coding genes, lncRNAs influence cardiac processes such as cell proliferation, apoptosis, fibrosis, and inflammation, contributing to the pathogenesis of various cardiac diseases, including heart failure, arrhythmias, ischemic disease, and cardiomyopathies.

This Special Issue aims to consolidate leading research on the diverse roles of lncRNAs in the epigenetic regulation of cardiac health and disease. We invite submissions on the following themes:

● Mechanisms of Action: Exploring how lncRNAs regulate transcription, chromatin remodeling, and post-transcriptional modifications in cardiac cells.

● lncRNAs and Cardiac Development: The role of lncRNAs in embryonic cardiac development and their potential impact on congenital heart defects.

● lncRNA-Mediated Pathways in Cardiac Remodeling: Insights into lncRNA involvement in hypertrophy, fibrosis, and metabolic reprogramming in heart disease.

● Biomarkers and Diagnostics: The potential of lncRNAs as non-invasive biomarkers for early detection and risk assessment in cardiovascular diseases.

● Therapeutic Strategies: Advancements in targeting lncRNAs for therapeutic purposes, including delivery methods and safety considerations.

● Interaction with Other ncRNAs: The interplay between lncRNAs and other non-coding RNAs, such as microRNAs, in cardiac pathology.

● Sex-Specific Roles of lncRNAs in Cardiac Health: Investigating how lncRNA expression and function may differ between sexes and impact disease susceptibility.


By bringing together these insights, we hope to foster a deeper understanding of lncRNAs as central players in cardiac epigenetics and stimulate advancements in their clinical application. This issue will serve as a valuable resource for researchers and clinicians striving to leverage lncRNAs to improve diagnostics, prognostics, and therapeutics in cardiovascular medicine.
keywords: ["Long Non-Coding RNAs", "lncRNAs", "cardiac diseases"]

topic: Topic: Data-driven Science And Trustworthy Machine Learning For Dynamic Systems
summary: Data-driven science and trustworthy machine learning techniques have become essential tools in advancing artificial intelligence (AI) for both scientific and engineering applications. One of the primary challenges in this field is extracting and understanding the physical properties and dynamic behaviors hidden within vast amounts of data from various engineering domains. Accurately identifying and modeling these dynamics is critical for effectively applying AI technologies. This Special Issue aims to bring together the latest theoretical advances in several key areas, including data-driven science for dynamic systems, interpretable and trustworthy machine learning techniques, and physics-informed learning approaches, as well as their engineering applications to the design and optimization of complex systems, advanced control strategies, condition monitoring, and system characterization, etc. We invite submissions across disciplines related to data-driven science and dynamic systems.
keywords: ["Trustworthy machine learning", "data-driven science", "interpretability", "dynamics"]

topic: Topic: Facial Flap Frontiers: Fresh Frameworks for Face Transplantation
summary:
This Special Issue, "Facial Flap Frontiers: Fresh Frameworks for Face Transplantation", delves into the latest advancements, challenges, and innovative techniques shaping the future of facial transplantation. With a strong interdisciplinary focus, this issue invites groundbreaking research, clinical case studies, and reviews from experts across surgical, regenerative, and rehabilitation domains. From the development of novel surgical techniques to ethical considerations, the issue aims to broaden our understanding of Face Transplantation.

Face transplantation requires precise surgical planning, advanced imaging, and meticulous microsurgical techniques. Recent advances in immunosuppression, tissue engineering, and donor-recipient matching have considerably expanded the potential for successful outcomes, offering improved functionality and appearance for individuals with severe facial deformities. However, these procedures also raise unique challenges regarding long-term care, psychological impact, and ethical considerations. This Special Issue will explore not only the technical aspects of face transplantation but also the holistic frameworks essential to patient-centered care, rehabilitation, and quality of life.

Through this Special Issue, we aim to provide a comprehensive resource that supports the ongoing advancement of facial transplantation as a viable, sustainable, and ethical solution for patients in need. We hope it will serve as an invaluable reference for surgeons, researchers, and healthcare providers dedicated to transforming lives through facial reconstruction.
keywords: ["Face transplantation", "reconstructive surgery", "microsurgery", "allotransplantation", "VCA"]

source: Journal of Surveillance, Security and Safety
topic: Topic: Cyber Security and Digital Forensics: Trends, Issues, and Challenges
summary:
As cyber threats continue to evolve, the fields of cyber security and digital forensics have become increasingly vital in safeguarding sensitive information and ensuring the integrity of digital systems. Cyber security encompasses a wide range of practices and technologies designed to protect networks, devices, and data from unauthorized access, attacks, and damage. Meanwhile, digital forensics focuses on the identification, preservation, analysis, and presentation of electronic evidence, playing a crucial role in investigating cyber crimes and ensuring accountability. The rapid evolution of technology and increasingly sophisticated cyber threats present numerous challenges for cybersecurity and digital forensics professionals. Emerging trends such as the rise of ransomware, the proliferation of Internet-of-Things (IoT) devices, and the complexity of cloud computing environments have heightened the need for innovative solutions and strategies. Additionally, issues surrounding privacy, regulatory compliance, and the ethical implications of digital investigations must be addressed to navigate the evolving landscape of cyber security and digital forensics effectively.

This Special Issue aims to gather the latest research and developments in the fields of cyber security and digital forensics, providing a comprehensive overview of current trends, issues, and challenges. Topics of interest include, but are not limited to:
● Threat Intelligence and Cyber Threat Hunting;
● Incident Response and Recovery Strategies;
● Forensic Analysis of IoT Devices;
● Malware Analysis and Reverse Engineering;
● Cyber Security Frameworks and Best Practices;
● Data Privacy and Protection Regulations;
● Digital Evidence Collection and Preservation;
● Machine Learning Applications in Cyber Security and Forensics;
● Challenges in Cloud Security and Forensics;
● Ethical and Legal Considerations in Digital Investigations;
● Emerging Trends in Cyber Crime and Cybersecurity.

We invite academics, research students, and professionals to submit their original work. Extended conference papers with at least 50% new content are also welcome.
keywords: ["Cybersecurity", "digital forensics", "data protection", "privacy"]

topic: Topic: Fate and Risks of Chemicals of Emerging Concern in a One Health Context
summary:
Chemicals of emerging concern (CECs) - including pharmaceuticals, personal care products, and industrial compounds - have become a major concern for human and environmental health. Increasingly detected in various ecosystems, these chemicals pose potential risks to health and biodiversity. This Special Issue addresses the multifaceted challenges of CEC exposure, promoting the One Health approach that emphasizes the interconnectedness of human, animal, and environmental health. By adopting this holistic perspective, this issue aims to enhance understanding of the exposure risks of CECs present to both people and ecosystems.

The articles in this issue will cover topics such as the pathways through which CECs enter, persist, and interact in the environment, the associated exposure risks, and the effects on human and animal health. Additionally, the issue will evaluate current regulatory and management strategies, identifying gaps and proposing improvements to mitigate the risks of CEC exposure. This comprehensive overview will provide updated scientific information and insights for scientists, policymakers, and practitioners. The goal is to foster informed discussions and inspire innovative solutions to safeguard the health of our planet and its inhabitants. We invite contributions that bring relevant scientific perspectives and findings to this Special Issue.

All submissions MUST address the implications for human and/or wildlife exposure to contaminants. For further guidance, please refer to the Aims and Scope of JEEA.
keywords: ["One health", "pollutant uptake pathways", "environmental pollutants", "exposure risk", "biomonitoring", "pollution sources", "contaminants"]

topic: Topic: MXene Materials: Design, Synthesis, and Applications
summary:
MXenes, a class of two-dimensional transition metal carbides, nitrides, and carbonitrides, are synthesized using a top-down method that selectively etches A-site elements from the MAX phases. The general formula for the MAX phase is Mn+1AXn, where M represents early transition metals (e.g., Ti, V, and Cr), A corresponds to group A elements (e.g., Si, Al, and Sn), and X denotes carbon and/or nitrogen, with n ranging from 1 to 4. MXenes exhibit remarkable properties such as high electronic conductivity and tunable surface terminations, making them highly promising for applications spanning energy storage, electromagnetic shielding, catalysis, biomedicine, electronics, environment, sensors, and more. 

Since the inaugural International Conference on MXenes in May 2018 in Changchun, China, the field has grown significantly, with five events having taken place by November 2024. This expansion reflects the escalating global interest in MXenes, evidenced by a surge in publications—totaling 3,244 articles in leading journals by mid-November 2024. Further underscoring their significance, MXenes have been recognized as one of the IUPAC's 2024 top ten emerging technologies in chemistry. 

This Special Issue aims to highlight groundbreaking advancements and cutting-edge research on MXenes and related materials. We invite submissions of Research Papers, Reviews, Perspectives, Mini Reviews, and other formats that explore the latest developments in design, synthesis, and applications.

Topics of interest include but are not limited to:

1. Advanced and sustainable strategies for synthesizing MXenes;

2. Structural and multifunctional design innovations;

3. Progress in applications such as energy storage, electromagnetic shielding, catalysis, biomedicine, electronics, environment, sensors, and beyond.
keywords: ["MXene", "energy storage", "electromagnetic shielding", "catalysis", "biomedicine", "environment", "microwave absorption"]

topic: Topic: Emerging Organic Contaminants in Water and Wastewater: Occurrence and Remediation
summary:
The prevalence of emerging organic contaminants (EOCs) in water and wastewater has become an important environmental issue. These contaminants, including pharmaceuticals, personal care products, endocrine-disrupting compounds, per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFASs), flame retardants, pesticides, and various industrial chemicals, may pose significant risks to ecosystems and human health. As conventional water and wastewater treatment methods often cannot completely eliminate these contaminants, there is an urgent need for innovative treatment methods. This Special Issue aims to explore the occurrence, fate, and effective treatment techniques for EOCs in aquatic environments. We invite submissions that present original research, critical reviews, or case studies addressing EOCs in water and wastewater.

Topics to be Covered:
1. Sources and Occurrence: Studies on the sources of EOCs, their pathways into aquatic systems, and their distribution in aquatic systems.
2. Analytical Methods: Development and application of advanced analytical techniques for the detection and quantification of EOCs at trace levels.
3. Environmental Impact: Investigations into the ecological and health impacts of EOCs, including their bioaccumulation and toxicity in aquatic organisms.
4. Remediation Technologies: Evaluation of conventional and novel treatment methods for the removal of EOCs, including physical, chemical, and biological approaches.   Evaluation of limitations of treatment methods.
5. Case Studies: Real-world examples of successful remediation projects and the challenges encountered in managing EOCs in water and wastewater.
6. Policies and Regulations: Analysis of existing regulations, policies, and their effectiveness in controlling EOCs, as well as recommendations for future policy development.
keywords: ["Emerging organic contaminants", "water treatment", "wastewater treatment", "environmental impact", "remediation technologies", "analytical methods", "ecological health"]

topic: Topic: Optimal Decision and Control of Intelligent Marine Vehicles
summary:
In recent years, intelligent marine vehicles have been a research hotspot due to their potential for autonomous operations, such as marine search and rescue, submarine laying, marine environmental monitoring, marine resource exploration, autonomous cruising, and maritime surveillance. However, several challenges remain in the theoretical investigation and practical implementation of these vehicles, including environmental awareness, path planning and decision, restricted communication resources, seagoing capability and security, and multi-vehicle cooperation.

This Special Issue aims to address these challenges by featuring cutting-edge studies that advance both the theoretical foundations and engineering applications of intelligent marine vehicles. We seek contributions that push the boundaries of optimal decision-making and control, focusing on novel approaches, innovative techniques and transformative applications. Potential topics include but are not limited to:
● Autonomous path-following control for marine vehicles;
● Cooperative formation control for multiple vehicles;
● Path planning and trajectory optimization for marine vehicles;
● Intelligent guidance and control with advanced techniques;
● Cross-domain collaboration for marine vehicles;
● Clean energy-driven intelligent navigation technologies;
● Deep reinforcement learning in maritime transport mission;
● Dynamic positioning and other advanced techniques in automatic navigation.
keywords: ["Intelligent control", "path-following control", "neural networks", "marine vehicles", "deep reinforcement learning"]

topic: Topic: Phosphorus Materials for Fast Charging Energy Storage
summary:
The advancement of energy storage technologies, notably fast-charging battery systems, is a critical area of research. Phosphorus-based materials have proven to be essential components, showcasing substantial promise in enhancing energy storage performance. The incorporation of phosphorus-based compounds into battery frameworks has unveiled diverse avenues for innovation, thereby expediting the evolution of high-speed charging solutions adaptable across diverse industries.

This Special Issue aims to underscore groundbreaking concepts in forging innovative electrode materials tailored for rapid-charging batteries. Topics of interest include the optimization of material structure and composition, surface treatment methods, electrode interfaces, and exploration of fundamental mechanisms. We invite submissions across multiple domains, spanning lithium-ion, sodium-ion, potassium-ion batteries, and supercapacitors. Original Research and Review articles are encouraged.
keywords: ["Lithium-ion batteries", "sodium-ion batteries", "potassium-ion batteries", "electrode material design", "reaction mechanism"]

topic: Topic: Modern Alchemy: Tailoring Materials for Energy Storage in Battery Systems
summary:
Given the challenges posed by the ongoing energy transition, together with the increasing digitalization and mobility of society, there is a growing demand for portable electronic devices. Additionally, the rapid adoption of electric vehicles, among other relevant application areas, underscores the need for advancements in energy storage systems. In this context, batteries stand out as one of the most promising solutions, with novel materials and material combinations driving technological progress. As such, researchers are not only focusing on improving battery performance but also on enhancing safety and reducing environmental impact.

In this rich and dynamic scenario, the Special Issue highlights the development of advanced batteries, featuring both theoretical and experimental approaches. We particularly welcome research on sustainable materials and performance improvements, along with innovative processing methods, including additive manufacturing, for battery system design and development.

This Special Issue aims to showcase the latest research, computational studies and reviews on materials for battery applications. Topics covered include, but are not limited to:
● Materials and processes for batteries systems;
● Sustainable materials for battery systems;
● Advanced computing methods, including machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI), for materials design, development, integration and battery performance;
● Design and production methods for battery devices;
● Smart capabilities of battery systems;
● Advanced characterization techniques applied to materials and interfaces;
● Technological breakthroughs and innovations;
● Environmental and economic aspects, including life cycle assessment.
keywords: ["Materials for batteries", "energy storage", "processing technologies", "battery chemistries", "advanced characterization", "materials integration", "system integration", "applications"]

topic: Topic: Sustainable 3D-printed Bio-inspired Structures and its Life Cycle Assessment
summary:
In today's era of growing environmental concerns, sustainable 3D-printed bio-inspired structures and their life cycle assessment (LCA) have become an important topic in additive manufacturing technology. With various environmental challenges, such as resource depletion and waste generation, additive manufacturing offers a potential solution by enabling the creation of complex geometries with minimal material waste. Bio-inspired structures, modeled after nature's efficient designs, can exhibit enhanced properties while reducing environmental impact. Additionally, the LCA of 3D-printed structures helps evaluate the entire life cycle of sustainable bio-based materials, which ranges from raw material extraction to end-of-life disposal. Sustainable 3D-printed bio-inspired structures have the potential to revolutionize various industries, from aerospace to healthcare, and contribute to a more sustainable future.

The Special Issue, "Sustainable 3D-printed Bio-inspired Structures and Its Life Cycle Assessment", explores the sustainability in additive manufacturing and involves the development of environmentally friendly materials and the optimization of energy-efficient and waste-reducing printing methods. It also examines the entire life cycle of 3D-printed products, from material sourcing and production to end-of-life management, including biodegradability and recyclability. The Special Issue covers a range of topics, including but not limited to:
● Development of biodegradable or renewable materials for additive manufacturing;
● Innovations in material recycling for additive manufacturing;
● Energy-efficient and low-waste 3D-printed processes;
● LCA of 3D-printed products;
● End-of-life management strategies, including biodegradability and recycling;
● Additive manufacturing in circular economy models;
● Process optimization for reduced environmental impact;
● Integration of renewable energy into additive manufacturing systems;
● Policy and regulatory frameworks for sustainable additive manufacturing.

Overall, the Special Issue provides valuable insights and practical guidance for researchers and engineers to evaluate the emerging practice of sustainable 3D-printed bio-inspired structures and their LCA with advanced additive manufacturing technologies.
keywords: ["Sustainability", "additive manufacturing", "3D-printed bio-inspired structure", "life cycle assessment", "environmental impact"]

topic: Topic: Machine Learning-enabled Exploration of Electrocatalysts for Energy Conversion Technology
summary:
This Special Issue of Journal of Materials Informatics, titled "Machine Learning-Enabled Exploration of Electrocatalysts for Energy Conversion Technology," showcases the pivotal role of machine learning in advancing electrocatalysis. By leveraging machine learning, the development and optimization of electrocatalysts critical for sustainable energy technologies have been revolutionized.

The articles featured in this issue cover a wide spectrum, from designing algorithms tailored to materials science to applying these methods in discovering and optimizing novel electrocatalytic systems. Contributors explore how machine learning facilitates the prediction of catalytic performance, identification of new electrocatalyst materials, and refinement of operational parameters, thereby streamlining experimental procedures and accelerating innovation.

This collection not only underscores the impactful integration of machine learning in electrocatalyst research but also outlines the future potential of these technologies to further revolutionize energy conversion efficiency and sustainability. Readers are invited to delve into these contributions to grasp the current trends and explore the future possibilities of machine learning in electrocatalysis.
keywords: ["Machine learning", "algorithm development", "electrocatalysis", "energy conversion", "sustainable technology"]

topic: Topic: Heterostructures for Superior Properties
summary:
Heterostructured materials have attracted significant attention in recent years due to their superior mechanical properties compared to their homogeneous counterparts. More importantly, they can be easily processed using existing industrial facilities on a large scale and at low cost, paving the way for commercial application. It has established hetero-structured materials as a prominent field of research within materials science. Despite these advancements, several scientific challenges remain, including understanding the fundamental principles of their exceptional properties, developing effective measurement strategies to quantify heterostructure effects, and formulating design guidelines.

This Special Issue seeks to present a comprehensive overview of recent developments in heterostructured materials, covering a diverse array of topics, including but not limited to:
● Theoretical frameworks and design principles for heterostructured materials;
● Mechanisms for the construction of hetero-microstructures;
● Strategies for multi-scale hierarchical tunning of heterostructured zones;
● Advanced fabrication processes and technologies for heterostructured materials;
● Mechanisms and pathways for optimizing the performance of heterostructured materials;
● Structure-performance relationships in heterostructured materials;
● Industrial exploration and feasibility of heterostructured materials;
● Future perspectives in the development of heterostructured materials.

topic: Topic: Global Reviews on Human Exposure to Emerging Contaminants: Sources, Pathways, and Health Effects
summary:
This Special Issue focuses on critical reviews in the field of human exposure to emerging contaminants. By synthesizing and summarizing recent research advancementsin this field, this special issue will provide a comprehensive overview of  the sources, exposure pathways, and health implications of exposure to important groups of emerging contaminants. The findings of this Special Issue will not only help the academic community identify knowledge gaps in exposure pathways and health risk assessments but also enhance our understanding of the broader implications of these contaminants for human health. We invite review articles that specifically address significant contaminants, such as per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances, plastic additives, personal care products, and microplastics, among others. The objective is to deliver valuable insights and data references for researchers, environmental health professionals, epidemiologists, and public health policymakers. Through this issue, we aim to inspire new directions in scientific research and provide a solid scientific foundation for relevant policy-making.


All submissions MUST consider the implications for human and/or wildlife exposure to contaminants. Please see the Aims and Scope of JEEA for further guidance.
keywords: ["Human exposure", "emerging contaminants", "per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances", "plastic additives", "personal care products", "microplastics"]

topic: Topic: Research Advances in the Control and Removal Strategies for PFAS Contaminants
summary:
Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) are persistent and pervasive environmental contaminants, widely known for their extreme resistance to degradation and potential to bioaccumulate in living organisms. The increasing presence of PFAS in water systems, soils, and even human tissues has sparked significant global concerns due to their adverse effects on ecosystems and human health. As regulatory bodies and research communities intensify their efforts to address these pressing challenges, there is a growing need for innovative strategies to control and remove PFAS from various environments.

This Special Issue, "Research Advances in the Control and Removal Strategies for PFAS Contaminants," compiles the latest research findings and technological innovations in PFAS management. We invite contributions that explore new methods for the detection, control, and removal of PFAS, with topics including but not limited to advanced oxidation processes, adsorption techniques, bioremediation, thermal treatment methods, the fate and transport of PFAS in different environmental matrices, and the development of regulatory frameworks and risk assessments. Submissions may take the form of research articles, reviews, systematic reviews, short communications, commentaries, editorials, letters to the editor, and perspectives. By enhancing our understanding of PFAS behavior in the environment and promoting the development of innovative remediation technologies, this issue aims to provide valuable insights for scientists and policymakers in the effective management of PFAS contamination.
keywords: ["PFAS contaminants", "water remediation", "advanced detection", "ecosystem health", "sustainable water treatment"]

topic: Topic: Metabolism: Molecular Mechanisms Underlying Cardiovascular Disease in the Elderly
summary:
The prevalence of cardiovascular disease (CVD) increases significantly with age. Dysregulation of cellular metabolism plays a pivotal role in cardiac aging and enhances susceptibility to CVD. Recent studies have uncovered intriguing insights into the metabolic alterations associated with CVD during aging. Understanding the molecular mechanisms underlying these metabolic perturbations is crucial for developing novel therapeutic strategies to combat aging-related CVD.

This Special Issue aims to explore the molecular mechanisms by which age-related metabolic changes, combined with sex, genetic, epigenetic, and environmental factors, drive the progression of CVD in the elderly. By fostering a comprehensive understanding of the intricate interplay between aging, metabolic alterations, and CVD, this issue seeks to identify innovative therapeutic targets and strategies. We welcome submissions in various formats, including Original Research, Reviews, Mini Reviews, and Perspectives. All contributions will undergo rigorous peer review to uphold the highest standards of quality and scientific integrity.

This Special Issue focuses on the following key topics:
1. Physiology and Pathology of Cardiac Metabolism in the Elderly.
Exploring the distinction between physiological metabolic adaptations and pathological changes in the aging heart. Investigations will address how metabolic remodeling contributes to maintaining cardiac function versus driving age-related cardiac decline.
2. Cell Type-specific Metabolic Alterations in the Aging Heart.
The heart is composed of diverse cell types, including atrial and ventricular cardiomyocytes, fibroblasts, endothelial cells, immune cells, and smooth muscle cells, each with distinct metabolic signatures. This topic investigates how metabolic changes in specific cell types interplay to influence cardiac aging and dysfunction.
3. Sex Differences in Cardiac Metabolism with Aging.
Examining how sex hormones regulate cardiac metabolism and how hormonal shifts during aging contribute to sex-specific differences in cardiovascular risk and outcomes.
4. Immunometabolism in the Elderly.
Investigating the interaction between inflammatory pathways and metabolic processes in aging populations, focusing on how these interactions affect cardiac health and contribute to age-related CVD.
5. Epigenetics, Epitranscriptomics, and Cardiac Metabolism in Aging.
Understanding the roles of epigenetic and epitranscriptomic modifications in shaping metabolic changes in the heart, and their implications for cardiac aging and disease progression.
keywords: ["Metabolism", "aging", "senescence", "cardiovascular disease", "sex difference", "inflammation; epigenetics", "epitranscriptomics", "physiology"]

source: Extracellular Vesicles and Circulating Nucleic Acids
topic: Topic: GISM Annual Meeting 2025: From MSCs to Extracellular Vesicles
summary:
The "Gruppo Italiano Staminali Mesenchimali (GISM)" is an organization comprising researchers from leading Italian universities and research associations. Its main purpose is to promote the exchange of knowledge and advancements in the field of mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs), with a particular emphasis on their clinical applications. On May 8-9, 2025, the GISM annual meeting will take place in Perugia (Italy) (https://www.gismonline.it/images/GISM2025/GISM2025_v20241003.pdf). Spanning two days, the meeting will feature a comprehensive program that includes sessions of interest to researchers involved in the production, application, and study of MSCs across clinical, veterinary, and broader research fields. A dedicated section will explore EVs and their application, from bench to bedside.

This EVCNA Special Issue intends to provide an up-to-date overview of advances in extracellular vesicle research, highlighting contributions from participants delivering oral presentations or posters at the GISM meeting. This collection will comprise full papers, review articles, and short communications covering the following EV topics:
- Molecular mechanisms of EV biogenesis and release;
- Roles of EVs in cell-cell communication and signaling pathways;
- Diagnostic and therapeutic potential of EVs;
- Advances in EV isolation and characterization techniques;
- Emerging clinical applications of EVs in various diseases.

The Special Issue will feature:
Conference Abstracts: Summaries of research presented during the GISM 2025 meeting.
Meeting Reports: Highlights and key takeaways from conference sessions and discussions, providing an overarching view of the event.
keywords: ["Mesenchymal stromal cells", "extracellular vesicles", "secretome", "oncology", "veterinary", "regenerative medicine"]

topic: Topic: AI for Chemistry
summary:
The integration of artificial intelligence (AI) into the chemical sciences is revolutionizing research, driving innovation and accelerating discovery across diverse subfields. Historically, chemical studies have been constrained by the complexity of vast parameter spaces, multi-dimensional optimization challenges, and the inefficiencies inherent in trial-and-error methodologies. AI is now providing solutions to these challenges, ushering in a new era of precision, predictive capabilities, and enhanced productivity.

This Special Issue, AI for Chemistry, aims to highlight the transformative power of AI in chemical research, showcasing advancements that are reshaping traditional practices and enabling new avenues of exploration in previously uncharted chemical domains. Key topics of focus include:
● AI-driven prediction of molecular and material properties;
● Machine learning-based mechanistic insights into reaction pathways;
● Development of autonomous systems for chemical synthesis and discovery;
● AI-guided design of catalysts and energy materials;
● Data-driven optimization in energy storage, pharmaceuticals, and sustainable chemistry;
● AI-driven computational methods for faster, more accurate simulations;
● Automated synthesis planning, including routes and conditions.

Beyond technical breakthroughs, this Special Issue will also examine the ethical and practical considerations of applying AI to chemical research, emphasizing best practices for ensuring responsible and impactful integration.

By featuring original research articles, comprehensive reviews, and forward-looking perspectives, this Special Issue seeks to foster interdisciplinary collaboration, promote the widespread adoption of AI methodologies, and inspire the development of intelligent tools for addressing critical challenges in the chemical sciences. AI for Chemistry aspires to be a nexus for transformative ideas, empowering the global research community to shape the future of chemistry with AI.
keywords: ["Artificial intelligence", "machine learning", "autonomous synthesis", "catalyst design", "property prediction", "data-driven optimization", "energy materials", "ethical integration"]

topic: Topic: Emerging Marine Pollutants Challenges and Solutions for a Sustainable Ocean
summary:
Over the past decades, numerous emerging pollutants have entered the water cycle due to extensive usage. The development of sensitive analytical methods has facilitated their identification and measurement in environmental samples, with toxicity tests revealing the magnitude of the issue. Pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, disinfection by-products, and micro- and nano-plastics are some key examples, while ongoing studies continue to identify new pollutants and their transformation products, particularly in the ocean.

This Special Issue focuses on studies concerning the development and optimization of methods for detecting emerging pollutants in marine waters and sediments, evaluating their environmental and toxic impacts, and exploring strategies for reducing their presence and mitigating their effects. We welcome original research, reviews, communications, and perspectives on all topics related to the detection, mitigation, and management of emerging pollutants for a sustainable marine environment.
keywords: ["Emerging pollutants", "marine environment", "detection", "toxicity", "sustainability"]

source: Extracellular Vesicles and Circulating Nucleic Acids
topic: Topic: Machine Learning/AI Assisted Development of High-Performance Alloys
summary:
High-performance alloys form the cornerstone of numerous advanced technologies, playing critical roles in aerospace, automotive, energy, biomedical, and other industries. The development of these alloys, which require exceptional mechanical, thermal, and chemical properties, has traditionally relied on meticulous design, precise microstructural control, and optimal processing techniques. However, the increasing complexity of alloy demands now calls for more efficient and innovative approaches.

Artificial intelligence (AI) has emerged as a game-changer in the field of materials science, revolutionizing the design and development of high-performance alloys. AI accelerates alloy discovery and optimization, providing powerful tools to identify complex correlations, optimize processes, and explore vast compositional-microstructural spaces with unprecedented efficiency. Notably, cutting-edge algorithms such as active learning, generative models, and large language models (LLM) have opened new avenues in alloy design. These advanced AI approaches not only reduce the cost and time of alloy development but also facilitate the discovery of novel alloys with exceptional properties, effectively bridging the gap between theoretical design and practical applications.

In this Special Issue, we focus on the transformative impact of AI and state-of-the-art algorithms on the design of high-performance alloys. Contributions will highlight how AI-driven methods can address critical challenges in alloy research and development. We welcome both original research articles and insightful reviews on topics including, but not limited to:

● Development of high-throughput experimental and computational methods for creating high-quality alloy databases/datasets;

● Development of LLM models for materials and their applications in high-performance alloys;

● Development of AI-driven laboratories for alloy preparation and characterization;

● AI-enabled predictions of phase stability, thermodynamic properties, and kinetic behaviors in alloys;

● AI-driven composition design and screening for high-performance alloys;

● AI-driven approaches for optimizing microstructure, process parameters, and mechanical/functional properties;

● AI-driven acceleration of numerical simulations in alloys, such as molecular dynamics, phase-field modeling, and others;

● AI-driven tools for the accelerated discovery of novel high-performance alloys;

● Applications of AI in predicting mechanical, thermal, and functional properties, as well as the performance of alloys;

● Integration of AI methods with first-principles calculations, molecular dynamics, CALPHAD modeling, phase-field simulations, or multi-scale simulations.
keywords: ["Machine learning", "artificial intelligence", "high-performance alloys", "alloy design and optimization", "predictive modeling"]

source: Journal of Translational Genetics and Genomics
topic: Topic: Proceedings of 3rd European Congress on Human Genetics
summary:
This Special Issue is dedicated to the "3rd European Congress on Human Genetics," which will be held in Vienna, Austria, from June 19 to 20, 2025. With the theme "Genetic Insights for a Healthier Tomorrow," the congress will showcase groundbreaking research, advancements, and discussions in the field of human genetics.

As a major academic event in the field of human genetics, the 3rd European Congress on Human Genetics provides a valuable platform for promoting interdisciplinary collaboration and innovation. Through selected papers from the conference, this Special Issue delves into the latest techniques in genomics, the discovery of disease biomarkers, and the potential of genetics in disease prediction and intervention. It aims to offer valuable insights and practical guidance for the global academic community and the clinical medical field.

This Special Issue covers research related to human genetics, including the following topics:

topic: Topic: Exploring the Mechanism of Metal Cluster Catalysis
summary:
Metal nanoclusters, with their unique electronic structures and quantum effects, exhibit outstanding performance in catalysis. This Special Issue focuses on the catalytic mechanisms of metal nanoclusters, exploring their size effects, surface/interface structures, and electronic/spin states to elucidate their regulation of reaction pathways, active sites, and energy barriers. The high density of under-coordinated sites and distinctive electronic states in nanoclusters significantly enhance the adsorption and activation of reactive species. Moreover, the tunability of nanoclusters enables the optimization of catalytic activity and selectivity through surface ligand regulation, metal-support interactions, and inter-cluster synergistic effects. This research provides theoretical support for uncovering the fundamental catalytic mechanisms of metal nanoclusters and lays the groundwork for designing efficient and sustainable catalytic systems for key energy catalytic reactions.

Specific areas to be covered include, but are not limited to:
● Quantum Size Effect;
● Surface Effects and Coordination Unsaturation;
● Charge Transfer and Activation Effects;
● Cluster-Support Synergistic Effects;
● Cluster Structure and Composition Modulation;
● Cluster Operando/in-situ Characterization;
● Paradigms of Nanocluster-Based Catalysis: Intact Nanoclusters for Catalysis; Locally Activated Nanoclusters for Catalysis; Ligand-off Nanoclusters for Catalysis; Nanoclusters as Precursors to New Catalysts.
keywords: ["Metal nanoclusters", "redox reaction", "electrocatalysis", "photocatalysis", "electron-transfer catalysis", "effect of ligands on the same metal core", "CO<sub>2</sub> reduction", "water electrolysis", "hydrogen production", "material advanced characterization", "ligand-on and -off effect", "nanoclusters material interdisciplinary"]

topic: Topic: Molecule-based Solid Materials in Advanced Electrocatalysis
summary:
The design of advanced solid catalysts is crucial for converting energy/environment-related small molecules and promoting global sustainability. In recent years, molecule-based solid materials (MSMs) featuring highly customizable organic units have demonstrated unique advantages in electrocatalytic chemistry, emerging as an attractive platform for advanced electrocatalyst design. MSMs, such as metal-organic frameworks (MOFs), covalent organic frameworks (COFs), porous organic polymers (POPs), etc., offer high specific surface areas, well-developed porosities, and readily adjustable structures. Compared to traditional inorganic materials, MSMs exhibit impressive performance and distinct catalytic behavior in heterogeneous electrocatalysis. To advance this field, it is essential to thoroughly understand the thermodynamics and kinetics to elucidate the physicochemical mechanisms of their electrocatalytic processes. Additionally, key scientific challenges include designing and synthesizing novel active sites, precisely controlling the structural parameters, and elucidating the molecular enhancement mechanisms. The Special Issue highlights recent progress in MSMs for electrochemical catalytic systems and explores their potential applications.

Specific topics include, but are not limited to:

● Synthesis and regulation of MSMs; 

● MSMs for designing advanced electrocatalysts;

● MSMs for electrocatalytic water splitting;

● MSMs for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction;

● MSMs for electrocatalytic small molecule conversion.
keywords: ["Molecule-based materials", "organic-inorganic hybrid materials", "metal-organic frameworks", "covalent organic frameworks", "porous organic polymers", "nanomaterials", "electrocatalysis", "small molecule activation", "energy conversion"]

topic: Topic: Advances in 2D Materials for Flexible Electronics
summary:
Flexible electronics are at the forefront of modern science and technology, offering broad applications in wearable devices, the Internet of Things (IoT), biomedical devices, and smart sensors. Two-dimensional (2D) materials, such as graphene, transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs), Black Phosphorus (BP), and MAXene, have become pivotal in this field. Their exceptional physicochemical properties, including high flexibility, superior electrical performance, and mechanical stability, enable groundbreaking advancements. This Special Issue highlights the latest developments in 2D materials for flexible electronics and covers material innovation, performance optimization strategies, device integration techniques, and potential applications in next-generation systems.

We invite original research and review articles that explore topics such as the synthesis of 2D materials and their applications in flexible electronic devices, including flexible sensors, flexible transistors, flexible logical devices, flexible energy devices, and flexible integrated circuits and functional systems. 

This platform aims to foster interdisciplinary collaboration and accelerate the industrialization of flexible electronics.
keywords: ["2D materials", "flexible electronics", "graphene", "MAXene", "flexible sensors", "wearable devices", "energy storage devices", "interface engineering", "flexible transistors", "flexible electronic systems"]

topic: Topic: Exploring Materials Informatics: Emerging Technologies Inspired by the 2024 Nobel Prize
summary:
The 2024 Nobel Prizes in Physics and Chemistry highlight the revolutionary advances in artificial intelligence (AI) technologies, which are revolutionizing many scientific disciplines, including materials science. Innovations in generative models, deep learning, and reinforcement learning are ushering in a new era in materials discovery, design, and optimization.

These AI-driven approaches are accelerating the development of next-generation materials and enabling the efficient, sustainable discovery of novel materials with tailored properties. In particular, multi-scale modeling and data-driven methodologies are providing deeper insights of material behavior across various scales, from atomic structures to large-scale applications.

This Special Issue celebrates and builds upon the transformative insights inspired by the 2024 Nobel Prizes, exploring the intersection of AI and materials science, and fostering innovations poised to redefine the future of materials research. We invite contributions from researchers and innovators in materials informatics and AI, with a focus on the following topics:
● AI for Materials, AI for Science, and AI for smart manufacturing;
● AI-enabled materials data acquisition and database construction;
● Machine learning models for material property prediction and optimization;
● Self-driving laboratories and experimental automation;
● Multi-scale modeling and simulations powered by AI;
● Data-driven approaches to uncovering fundamental material behaviors;
● Large language models (LLM) and AI agents, along with their applications in material design and manufacturing.
keywords: ["Artificial intelligence (AI)", "materials informatics", "machine learning", "database construction", "experimental automation", "Nobel Prize 2024"]

topic: Topic: Genomic Insights into the Etiology of Autism Spectrum Disorder in Children
summary:
Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a multifaceted neurodevelopmental condition marked by variability in social communication, behavior, and cognitive functions. Recent advancements in genomic research have shed light on the genetic underpinnings of ASD, offering promising pathways for understanding its complex etiology. This Special Issue aims to highlight cutting-edge research that delves into the genetic and epigenetic factors influencing ASD, with a particular focus on children.

We welcome contributions in the form of original research, comprehensive reviews, and case studies on topics including, but not limited to:

● Genetic Variants and Risk Genes: Identification and functional analysis of genetic variants linked to ASD susceptibility;

● Epigenetic Modifications: The role of DNA methylation, histone modifications, and non-coding RNAs in ASD;

● Gene-Environment Interactions: How environmental exposures influence genetic predisposition and contribute to ASD onset;

● Whole-Genome and Exome Sequencing: Advances in next-generation sequencing technologies for identifying novel mutations in ASD;

● Genetic Syndromes Associated with ASD: Insights into syndromic forms of autism and their genetic basis;

● Biomarkers and Diagnostics: Genomic markers for early diagnosis and prognosis of ASD in children;

● Therapeutic Implications: The potential of gene-based and personalized therapies in ASD management;

● Neurogenomics and Brain Development: Genomic studies linking gene expression to neurodevelopmental pathways in ASD;

● Inherited vs. De Novo Mutations: The role of inherited mutations and spontaneous genetic changes in ASD;

● Genetic Counseling: Implications of genomic findings for family planning and genetic counseling in ASD.
keywords: ["Autism spectrum disorder", "genomic research", "genetic variants", "epigenetics", "gene-environment interaction", "whole-genome sequencing", "biomarkers"]

topic: Topic: Advancements in Gene Editing for Hereditary Hearing Loss
summary:
Hereditary hearing loss is one of the most prevalent genetic disorders, with significant implications for communication and quality of life. Recent breakthroughs in gene editing technologies, such as CRISPR-Cas9 and base editing, have opened new avenues for correcting genetic mutations responsible for hearing impairment. This Special Issue focuses on exploring innovative gene editing approaches to address hereditary hearing loss and their potential for clinical translation.

We invite submissions of original research, reviews, and case reports covering a wide range of topics, including but not limited to:

● Gene Editing Technologies: Advances in CRISPR-Cas9, base editing, and prime editing for auditory genes;

● Target Genes and Mutations: Identification and editing of genes associated with hereditary hearing loss, such as GJB2, MYO7A, and TMC1;

● Delivery Methods: Development of efficient delivery systems for gene therapies, including viral and non-viral vectors targeting the inner ear;

● Precision Medicine: Personalized approaches to gene therapy based on individual genetic profiles;

● Ethical and Regulatory Considerations: Ethical issues and regulatory challenges in applying gene editing for hearing loss;

● Preclinical and Clinical Studies: Animal models and clinical trials assessing the safety and efficacy of gene editing therapies;

● Gene-Environment Interactions: How environmental factors may influence the outcomes of gene therapy for hearing loss;

● Regeneration and Repair: Gene editing for hair cell regeneration and cochlear repair;

● Multigenic and Syndromic Hearing Loss: Approaches to editing complex genetic disorders that include hearing loss as a component.
keywords: ["Hereditary hearing loss", "gene editing", "CRISPR-Cas9", "base editing", "gene therapy"]

topic: Topic: Role of Immune Cells in the Tumor Microenvironment Ⅱ
summary:
The tumor microenvironment (TME) is a dynamic ecosystem in which immune cells play crucial roles in tumor progression, immune evasion, and therapy resistance. This Special Issue, Role of Immune Cells in the Tumor Microenvironment II, focuses on the complex interactions between immune cells and the TME, exploring their impact on cancer development and therapeutic outcomes. We aim to provide insights into immune modulation strategies, including immune checkpoint inhibition, CAR-T cell therapy, and novel approaches to overcoming immune suppression in cancer treatment.

We invite research articles and reviews that explore topics including, but not limited to:

● Tumor-associated macrophages, dendritic cells, and myeloid-derived suppressor cells in immune evasion;

● Mechanisms of immune suppression and resistance to therapy;

● Immune checkpoint inhibition and combination immunotherapies;

● Tumor-immune interactions in metastasis;

● Novel biomarkers and immune reprogramming strategies.

topic: Topic: The Impact of Bisphenol Exposure on Human Health
summary:
Bisphenol A (BPA), a typical endocrine-disrupting compound, is widely present and poses potential risks, leading to its regulation or restriction in many countries. Subsequently, various alternatives have emerged, including Bisphenol AF (BPAF), Bisphenol AP (BPAP), Bisphenol B (BPB), and so on. These chemicals can be released into the environment during production and use, entering the human body through water, food, atmosphere, and other pathways. However,  they have been proven to have adverse effects on organisms, including estrogenic effects and disruption of the endocrine system. Therefore, research into the occurrence of such chemicals in the environment, along with the resultant human exposure and health risks, is of great interest. 

This Special Issue aims to provide a comprehensive overview of the latest developments in the field of Bisphenols, covering their occurrence, exposure, risk, and toxicity. All papers MUST address, to some extent, the assessment of Bisphenol exposure. Relevant study topics include, but are not limited to:

1. New analytical methods for the measurement of Bisphenols;

2. Occurrence of Bisphenols in the environment;

3. Human exposure and associated health risks;

4. Toxicity effects of Bisphenols and associated mechanisms. 

All submissions MUST consider the implications for human and/or wildlife exposure to these contaminants. For further guidance, please refer to the Aims and Scope of JEEA.
keywords: ["Bisphenol", "risk assessment", "toxicity mechanism", "human exposure", "environmental matrices"]
